companyDescription:
Traditional banks? Slow, complicated, and full of roadblocks. Qred? Fast, flexible, and built for small business owners who don’t have time for bureaucracy. 

We started in 2015 because small businesses simply deserve better. While banks buried entrepreneurs in paperwork, we built financing solutions that actually work - quick, simple, and designed for doers.

Now, we’re a market leader across Northern Europe, helping businesses get the funding they need to grow, without the headaches. Whether it’s a business loan or a company credit card, we move at the speed of our customers’ ambitions, because their success can’t wait - and neither should their business funding.

50,000+ entrepreneurs supported
10 new jobs created every day
Top-rated on Trustpilot
7 markets

We’re not just another bank. We’re the bank that actually gets it done. 

Ready to grow with us? We’re always looking for great people - whether to join our team or partner with us. Explore our current career opportunities or send us an open application. 

Know entrepreneurs who need funding? Partner with Qred, earn rewards for successful referrals, and help more businesses get the financing they deserve. 

Let’s reshape small business financing together!
companySlogan: Powering entrepreneurs.
descriptionHtml:
Dedicated to powering entrepreneurs, Qred is a profitable, fast-growing fintech scale-up bank. In just 10 years, we’ve grown from startup to scale-up, now generating over €100 million in annual revenue, supporting more than 50,000 entrepreneurs, and helping create 10,000+ jobs each year. We combine smart tech with real-world experience to make funding simple, fast, and fair for entrepreneurs who need it most. With strong backing, a flat organization, and bold growth plans, We're now looking for a Legal Counsel who wants to kickstart their career in a dynamic, tech-driven environment.

About The Role

This is an ideal opportunity for someone who has recently graduated, or an excellent starting point for those looking to work part-time alongside their law studies. You’ll collaborate with senior legal experts, supporting Qred’s daily operations and regulatory requirements. It’s a broad, hands-on role where you’ll gain exposure to areas like commercial contracts, regulatory compliance, product development, and corporate governance.

You’ll report directly to our Chief Legal Officer and be part of a proactive Legal & Compliance team that works closely with the business.

What You’ll Do

  • Oversee legal and regulatory requirements across diverse practice areas, including corporate law, privacy law, labor law, marketing law, regulatory filings and information requirements, contract law and IP law.
  • Perform legal research, draft legal assessments and memos, and conduct gap analysis to support the business across all functions.
  • Draft and implement internal policies, instructions and routines to meet regulatory requirements.
  • Draft and review various commercial contracts, data processing agreements, and partnership agreements.
  • Provide advice on relevant regulatory requirements and implementation procedures, with a focus on AML and GDPR in particular.
  • Monitor and communicate ongoing legislative and regulatory changes.
  • Support key administrative tasks, including communicating with authorities and related reporting.
  • Contribute to the legal aspects of product development and implementation, as well as geographic expansion.


Who You Are

Our teammates are our most important asset and the foundation for our success. Traits we see as crucial in this position are independence, integrity, credibility, and self-motivation. To succeed in this role, you should be pragmatic and solutions-oriented with strong organizational skills and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.

We’re looking for someone in the very early stages of their legal career:

  • You recently graduated with a law degree (or will graduate shortly).
  • You have 0 to 2 years of legal experience.
  • You’re ambitious, solution-oriented, and eager to learn.
  • You thrive in a fast-paced, innovative tech environment.
  • You’re a true team player with a can-do attitude.
  • You’re fluent in Swedish and English, both written and spoken.


Why join us?

This is a unique opportunity to join a growing fintech that’s reshaping financial services for small businesses across Northern Europe. You’ll have the chance to grow, take ownership, and help build something meaningful, while working alongside a supportive and experienced Legal & Compliance team.

One last thing

This position is based at our headquarters in Stockholm, Sweden. We’re reviewing applications on a rolling basis, and while the start date is flexible, the right candidate can join us immediately.

At Qred, we believe hiring is a two-way street. Of course, we want to learn more about your background and experience, but we also care about what you’re looking for in your next step. That’s why in the first step of the application, we’ll ask a few short questions about what matters most to you. It’s quick and informal and helps us ensure this could be a great fit for both sides.

Qred celebrates diversity and does not discriminate based on ethnicity, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability status, or any other applicable characteristics protected by law.

Qred is an international FinTech scale-up that specializes in digital financing solutions for small businesses, with operations in the Nordics, Benelux, Germany and Brazil. We are passionate about helping small businesses thrive and grow, by providing them with business loans and credit cards. Right now we’re in an exciting phase of our journey that focuses on growth through smart product development and technology fueled by international expansion.

descriptionHtml:
Är du en engagerad och driven compliance-expert som brinner för säkerhet? Vill du arbeta i ett samarbetsinriktat och prestigelöst team där du får möjlighet att växa, påverka och utvecklas? Då vill vi gärna träffa dig!

Vi på B3 Secure är ett gäng passionerade säkerhetsexperter som hjälper företag och myndigheter att navigera i en allt mer komplex regulatorisk miljö. Vi växer och letar nu efter fler härliga kollegor som vill vara med och bygga vidare på vårt starka och inkluderande säkerhetserbjudande.

Vad du kan förvänta dig hos oss

Hos oss får du spännande och utvecklande uppdrag hos kunder som värdesätter din expertis. Vi tror på frihet, flexibilitet och gemenskap, och vi vet att du gör ditt bästa arbete när du trivs. Därför erbjuder vi en kultur där vi lär av varandra, har roligt på jobbet och ser till att du får det stöd du behöver för att lyckas.

Hur du kommer att bidra

Som compliance-konsult inom säkerhet kommer du att ha en rådgivande och ledande roll, där du hjälper kunder att säkerställa efterlevnad av viktiga lagar och regelverk. Dina arbetsuppgifter kan bland annat inkludera:

  • Driva arbetet med säkerhet by design och säkerhet by default
  • Säkerställa efterlevnad av regulatoriska krav och standarder inom NIS, NIS2, DORA, GDPR eller andra relevanta regelverk
  • Genomföra mognads-, risk- och GAP-analyser
  • Utforma och implementera policyer och processer för att hjälpa kunder att ligga steget före i compliance-arbetet
  • Löpande utvärdera verksamheters regelefterlevnad och ge vägledning kring riskhantering

Vi tror att du trivs i en rådgivande roll, där du skapar förtroende hos kunder och kollegor genom din expertis och din förmåga att förklara komplexa krav på ett tydligt och lättförståeligt sätt.

Är vi en bra match?

Vi letar efter dig som:

  • Har minst fyra års erfarenhet av att arbeta med compliance
  • Har kunskap inom compliance-risker, operativa risker och affärsrisker
  • Har erfarenhet av regelverk som NIS, NIS2, DORA eller GDPR
  • Du talar svenska flytande, då det krävs i det dagliga arbetet
  • Är en teamspelare som gillar att samarbeta och dela kunskap

Vad du får hos oss

En arbetsplats där vi värdesätter frihet, gemenskap och balans

  • En trygg anställning med schyssta villkor, konkurrenskraftig lön och fina förmåner
  • En härlig wellness-satsning där vi tränar, peppar och inspirerar varandra
  • Möjlighet att utbilda dig och certifiera dig inom det du brinner för
  • Ett team där vi hjälper varandra att bli bättre – och har kul längs vägen!

En arbetsplats som gör skillnad:

Vi är stolta över att ha utsetts till ett av Sveriges fem främsta karriärföretag 2025 – en utmärkelse som speglar vår energi, nyfikenhet och vårt engagemang för att skapa en inkluderande och inspirerande arbetsplats. Här värdesätts olika perspektiv och idéer, och vi tror att det är just det som gör oss starkare.

Hos oss får du friheten att styra över dina uppdrag, din arbetstid och din utveckling. Vi erbjuder konkurrenskraftiga löner, flexibilitet att forma din vardag och en sund balans mellan jobb och fritid. Och vi vet att gemenskap är minst lika viktigt som teknik – därför satsar vi på allt från konferensresor och sommarfester till sköna AW:s där vi kan släppa jobbet för en stund.

Om B3 Consulting Group

B3 Consulting Group är ett expansivt konsultbolag med cirka 1000 medarbetare. Vi brinner för innovation och hjälper Sveriges ledande företag och organisationer att skapa framtidens möjligheter genom digital transformation. B3 finns på tolv orter i Sverige samt i Polen, med huvudkontor i Stockholm. Bolaget grundades 2003, är noterat på Nasdaq Stockholm Small Cap och omsatte 1 141 MSEK 2023.

Vi är stolta över vår prisbelönta företagskultur, där olikheter, erfarenheter och gemensam energi värdesätts. Som ett etiskt och transparent bolag strävar vi efter att ha en positiv påverkan på samhället, människor och miljön. Vi är även engagerade i Women in Tech och initiativ för att stärka AI-utbildning för kvinnor. 2024 utsågs vi till Årets Employer Branding-företag, och vår HR-chef vann titeln Årets HR-chef.

Nyfiken?

Vi hoppas det! Hör av dig så pratar vi mer – vi ser fram emot att lära känna dig. 💙

descriptionHtml:
About Us

At FDJ UNITED, we don't just follow the game, we reinvent it.

FDJ UNITED is one of Europe’s leading betting and gaming operators, with a vast portfolio of iconic brands and a reputation for technological excellence. With more than 5,000 employees and a presence in around fifteen regulated markets, the Group offers a diversified, responsible range of games, both under exclusive rights and open to competition. We set new standards, proving that entertainment and safety can go hand in hand. Here, you’ll work alongside a team of passionate individuals dedicated to delivering the best and safest entertaining experiences for our customers every day.


We’re looking for bold people who are eager to succeed and ready to level-up the game. If you thrive on innovation, embrace challenges, and want to make a real impact at all levels, FDJ UNITED is your playing field.

Join us in shaping the future of gaming. Are you ready to LEVEL-UP THE GAME?


The role

We are looking for a Regulatory & Compliance Officer to join our Regional Legal & Regulatory team, with a primary focus on the Swedish and Danish markets.

Operating in a highly regulated environment, you will help ensure that FDJ UNITED not only meets but exceeds compliance expectations. You'll work closely with internal teams and regulators to turn regulatory complexity into business opportunities, while safeguarding our operations and reputation. This position reports directly to the Regional Head of Legal and Regulatory.


What you will do

  • Drive compliance strategy and translate FDJ UNITED's compliance vision into measurable outcomes, ensuring excellence in Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC).
  • Stay ahead of regulatory, legislative, and political developments, providing clear guidance and adapting strategies accordingly.
  • Identify risks early, streamline compliance workflows, and implement effective solutions to increase efficiency.
  • Collaborate across teams, fostering strong relationships to address compliance challenges proactively.
  • Manage licensing, audits, and maintain constructive relationships with regulators.
  • Oversee investigations, inquiries, and reporting obligations with accuracy and professionalism.


Staying compliant in your role:

  • Ensure that you adhere to the Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) obligations for your role.
  • Identify and raise any non-compliance incidents promptly to your line manager.
  • Challenge processes, policies and projects that will negatively impact compliance within the Group.
  • Complete all mandatory compliance training assigned to you.
  • Reach out to the Compliance Teams if unsure of any of your compliance obligations or the requirements are unclear.


How will success be measured in this role

  • Acting in line with FDJ UNITED values
  • Successful completion of all relevant training and other compliance activities that support FDJ's sustainable and responsible growth


Your experience

  • 3–5 years in a regulatory, legal, or compliance role within a highly regulated sector (e.g. fintech, banking, gambling, or digital services).
  • Demonstrated ability to engage with government authorities and manage regulatory inquiries, audits, or investigations.
  • Exposure to areas such as GRC frameworks, AMLD, compliance management, governance, or regulatory quality assurance.
  • Degree in law, business, or equivalent compliance/governance qualification preferred.
  • Business-level English is required. Proficiency in Swedish or Danish.
  • Analytical, pragmatic, and proactive – with the ability to balance regulatory demands with commercial objectives.


Our Way Of Working

Our world is hybrid.

A career is not a sprint. It’s a marathon. One of the perks of joining us is that we value you as a person first. Our hybrid world allows you to focus on your goals and responsibilities and lets you self-organise to improve your deliveries and get the work done in your own way.


Application Process

We believe talent knows no boundaries. Our hiring process focuses solely on your skills, experience, and potential to contribute to our team. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and evaluate each candidate based on merit, regardless of personal characteristics as the age, gender, origin, religion, sexual orientation, neurodiversity or disability.

companyDescription:
SJR in Sweden (SJR) är ett av Sveriges ledande och mest erfarna bolag inom rekrytering och konsultlösningar. Vår grundläggande idé är att hjälpa företag med rätt kompetens och människor med rätt utmaning. Vi skapar möjligheter för människor att utvecklas, och därmed organisationer att växa hållbart. SJR har blivit utnämnt som ett Karriärföretag 2024. 

Ända sedan starten 1993 har vi varit specialiserade inom såväl personlighetsbedömning som de områden vi rekryterar till, vilket ger oss en unik förmåga att utifrån högt ställda krav matcha rätt kompetens med rätt uppdragsgivare. Vi erbjuder specialistkompetens inom ekonomi och finans, HR och lön, Supply Chain och inköp, CSRD- och hålllbarhetsroller, IT och hybrid och chefsroller.

SJR är idag cirka 350 medarbetare och konsulter. Vi jobbar med kunder, konsulter och kandidater över hela Sverige – med kontor i Stockholm, Uppsala, Göteborg, Malmö och Helsingborg.
companySlogan: Ett av Sveriges ledande och mest erfarna konsult- och rekryteringsbolag. Vi skapar trygghet för er!
descriptionHtml:
Vill du bidra i arbetet med att motverka finansiell brottslighet och har erfarenhet inom transaktionsmonitorering? Då är du den vi söker!


Om tjänsten

Vår kund är ett av Nordens ledande finansbolag som på den svenska marknaden har ett brett utbud av finanseringstjänster gentemot både företag och privatpersoner genom leasing och avbetalning. Kunden sitter centralt i Stockholms City och du kommer ingå i ett AML team om 18 personer. Önskade start är omgående och 8 månader framåt med goda chanser till förlängning.


Ansvarsområden

I din roll som AML- analytiker kommer du vara en viktig del i arbetet med utredningar kopplade till KYC och AML. Du kommer främst att arbeta med transaktionsmonitorering och rapportering till myndigheter. Arbetet innefattar med att utreda transaktioner som avviker från de normala för vidare åtgärd. Du kommer att rapportera misstänkta transaktioner vidare internt som en del av kundens arbete med att bekämpa ekonomisk brottslighet.


Lämplig bakgrund

Vi söker dig som har erfarenhet av transaktionsmonitorering/övervakning inom AML/KYC-området. Vi lägger stor vikt vid personliga egenskaper och söker dig som är positiv, serviceinriktad och ansvarsfull samtidigt som du som har förmågan att skapa förtroende, arbeta strukturerat och leverera en god service. Du trivs i ett högt tempo och är en lagspelare inriktad på det gemensamma resultatet. Vidare är du bra på att kommunicera både i tal och skrift.


Ansökan

För mer information om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta Atena Kamaliha. Vi intervjuar löpande och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan ansökningstiden har gått ut. Sista ansökningsdag är 2025-10-02.

Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!
descriptionText: Vill du bidra i arbetet med att motverka finansiell brottslighet och har erfarenhet inom transaktionsmonitorering? Då är du den vi söker!Om tjänstenVår kund är ett av Nordens ledande finansbolag som på den svenska marknaden har ett brett utbud av finanseringstjänster gentemot både företag och privatpersoner genom leasing och avbetalning. Kunden sitter centralt i Stockholms City och du kommer ingå i ett AML team om 18 personer. Önskade start är omgående och 8 månader framåt med goda chanser till förlängning.AnsvarsområdenI din roll som AML- analytiker kommer du vara en viktig del i arbetet med utredningar kopplade till KYC och AML. Du kommer främst att arbeta med transaktionsmonitorering och rapportering till myndigheter. Arbetet innefattar med att utreda transaktioner som avviker från de normala för vidare åtgärd. Du kommer att rapportera misstänkta transaktioner vidare internt som en del av kundens arbete med att bekämpa ekonomisk brottslighet.Lämplig bakgrundVi söker dig som har erfarenhet av transaktionsmonitorering/övervakning inom AML/KYC-området. Vi lägger stor vikt vid personliga egenskaper och söker dig som är positiv, serviceinriktad och ansvarsfull samtidigt som du som har förmågan att skapa förtroende, arbeta strukturerat och leverera en god service. Du trivs i ett högt tempo och är en lagspelare inriktad på det gemensamma resultatet. Vidare är du bra på att kommunicera både i tal och skrift.AnsökanFör mer information om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta Atena Kamaliha. Vi intervjuar löpande och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan ansökningstiden har gått ut. Sista ansökningsdag är 2025-10-02.Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!
title: AML analytiker

companyDescription:
Independent Nordic investment bank 

ABG Sundal Collier is a Nordic investment bank, publicly quoted and listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange (ticker: ABG).

ABGSC provides superior distribution of Nordic securities to local and international investors. The combination of global reach, top ranked research and high quality corporate finance advisory services has established us as a preferred supplier in the markets where we compete.

ABGSC was established in 2001 through the merger of Sundal Collier & Co (founded in 1984) and ABG Securities (founded in 1997). With dual strength in investment research and corporate advisory work in the Nordic region, we quickly established ourselves as an important force in the Nordic investment banking sector.

The partnership currently includes approximately more than half of the company’s staff as partner-shareholders. This group controls more than a third of the company’s fully diluted equity and is committed to a partnership agreement that restricts how shares can be sold. We believe that this structure helps to ensure that key members of ABGSC have an important economic stake in the success of the entire enterprise.

The Group has two operating divisions (Markets and Investment Banking), a global research and a global support structure. As of the end of 2023, we have ∼330 partners and employees working from offices in Oslo, Stockholm, Copenhagen, London, Frankfurt, New York and Singapore.
companySlogan: Independent Nordic investment bank
descriptionHtml:
Become a part of ABGSC

ABG Sundal Collier is the independent Nordic investment bank, developed over 40 years, founded on an inclusive partnership culture and the ability to attract and develop top talent. Our purpose is to enable businesses and capital to grow and perform and our vision is to be the Nordic investment bank of choice.

 

Investment banking is all about understanding companies, industries and markets. Our core offering to clients is that we offer the best advice – derived from diligent analysis in a dynamic, high paced and competitive environment.

 

Our more than 330 partners and employees are located in the Nordic offices of Oslo, Stockholm and Copenhagen, in addition to our international offices in London, New York, Frankfurt, Singapore and Lucerne. 

 

At ABGSC, teamwork and collaboration are at the core of how we do business. We don’t believe in rigid hierarchies, titles, or standardised career ladders. Instead, we focus on results, recognising and rewarding individuals for their impact and achievements, regardless of tenure.

 

Our flat organisation is defined by openness, trust, and the freedom to share ideas. Across geographies and functions, we operate as one team. We value each other’s unique skills and perspectives, and together we work towards a shared purpose, vision, and set of values.

 

By joining ABGSC, you will become part of a persistent and dedicated team, united in striving for excellence and where every contribution makes a difference.

 

For this role you will be part of the Swedish compliance team based in Stockholm. 


Background and purpose of the role

As our new Compliance Officer, you will play a pivotal role in shaping and developing the recently launched Private Banking business area. The position requires both strategic insight and hands-on execution, with responsibility for translating regulatory requirements into effective processes, procedures, and governance frameworks. Success in the role requires a strong grasp of both the business’ operational realities and the regulatory framework governing Private Banking.

 

Candidate Profile

We are seeking an experienced and dedicated Compliance Officer with expertise in securities regulation and financial advisory services, eager to play a key role in shaping our Private Banking business into a market leader in Sweden.

 

The role has a primary focus on Private Banking but also carries broad responsibility for supporting and managing compliance matters across the entire organisation. Close collaboration with the business is essential, requiring a combination of integrity, sound judgment, and a pragmatic, solution-oriented approach. Strong interpersonal skills, the ability to build trust, and the capacity to balance regulatory demands with business needs are all critical.

 

Responsibilities:

  • Support and challenge the business by providing advice on securities-related regulations, particularly within the Private Banking area
  • Actively contribute to the development of Private Banking by translating regulatory requirements into practical solutions and drafting internal governance documents, policies, and procedures
  • Conduct ongoing compliance monitoring, risk assessments, and training activities
  • Serve as a trusted partner to the business and act as a bridge between commercial operations and the control functions, clarifying regulatory requirements while maintaining a business-oriented perspective

 

We are looking for an individual who:

  • Has several years of experience in compliance within the securities sector, ideally with a background in private banking, wealth management, or similar
  • Has excellent understanding of relevant regulations, including those related to investment advice, portfolio management, conflicts of interest, inducements, and fair advisory practices
  • Is experienced in working closely with the business, providing clear, solution-oriented guidance while maintaining the independence required of the compliance function
  • Has a strong ability to communicate complex information in a structured, accessible, and audience-tailored way
  • Is communicative, pragmatic, and down-to-earth, with the ability to build trust across the organisation
  • Thrives in a dynamic and evolving environment


Personal qualities:

  • Commercially aware yet confident in upholding regulatory requirements and the independence of the compliance function
  • Proactive in identifying risks at an early stage and proposing practical, feasible solutions
  • Structured and systematic, delivering high-quality results even under time pressure
  • Demonstrates high integrity and builds long-term, trust-based relationships internally and externally
  • Capable of explaining complex regulatory frameworks in a clear and engaging manner


If you have any questions regarding the role or our offer, please contact Andreas Lennegren, Head of Compliance Sweden at andreas.lennegren@abgsc.se.

descriptionHtml:
Head of Legal / General Counsel – Stockholm First North NASDAQ listed Tech Company

Excellent Salary

Sweden (Remote)


Our client is listed on the Stockholm First North NASDAQ and is a fast-growing technology provider to the online casino industry. Their technology is supplied globally, with strong distribution across Europe, North America, LATAM and Asia. The business is part of an international group and is now quickly scaling up their European operations and has major ambitions for growth and expansion.


ROLE RESPONSIBILITIES


We are seeking an experienced and highly skilled Head of Legal with a strong background in Swedish corporate law and stock market regulations to join our client’s team.


As the Head of Legal, you will lead a team of three and oversee all legal and compliance functions for the company. Reporting to the CFO, you will serve as a strategic advisor to the executive team and board, ensuring the company operates within the legal frameworks of the jurisdictions it serves. You will also play a critical role in compliance with Nasdaq regulations, MAR, AML, GDPR, gaming license requirements, and industry-specific standards, while overseeing capital raising efforts, M&A transactions, and other corporate activities.


Legal Strategy and Leadership

  • Develop and implement the company’s global legal strategy in alignment with business goals.
  • Provide legal guidance to the board, executives, and management teams on corporate governance, risk management, and regulatory compliance.

Regulatory Compliance

  • Ensure compliance with Swedish Nasdaq listing requirements and securities laws.
  • Monitor and enforce adherence to Market Abuse Regulation (MAR), Anti-Money Laundering (AML) regulations, and General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
  • Oversee compliance with gaming license obligations in multiple jurisdictions, ensuring the company adheres to all regulatory and operational requirements.

Capital Raising and Corporate Transactions

  • Advise on capital raising activities, including equity, debt offerings, and private placements.
  • Oversee the legal aspects of corporate transactions, including mergers and acquisitions (M&A), joint ventures, and restructuring efforts.
  • Draft, review, and negotiate transaction documents, ensuring compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

Gaming License Management

  • Lead and manage the application, renewal, and maintenance of gaming licenses in existing and new markets.
  • Establish internal policies and processes to ensure ongoing compliance with jurisdictional gaming regulations.
  • Serve as the primary point of contact with regulatory authorities regarding licensing and compliance matters.

Litigation and Dispute Resolution

  • Manage disputes, litigation, and arbitration processes.
  • Collaborate with external counsel as needed to resolve complex legal challenges.
  • Provide proactive guidance to mitigate potential disputes and legal risks.

Contract Management

  • Oversee drafting, negotiation, and management of contracts with partners, clients, and suppliers.
  • Ensure contracts align with legal, financial, and business goals.

Corporate Governance

  • Manage board and shareholder meeting documentation, ensuring compliance with corporate governance standards.
  • Provide legal support and advice to the board of directors and executive leadership.

Risk Management

  • Proactively identify, assess, and mitigate legal, regulatory, and reputational risks.
  • Establish frameworks for managing compliance with GDPR, MAR, AML, gaming licenses, and other legal requirements.

Team Leadership

  • Lead and grow an internal legal team or manage external counsel relationships.


SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE REQUIRED

To be considered for this exciting opportunity you must meet the following requirements


  • Education: LL.M. or JD from an accredited law school; Swedish or EU legal qualifications preferred.
  • Experience: Minimum 10 years of legal experience, with 5+ years in a senior legal role.
  • Experience in the gambling, gaming, or technology sector is highly desirable.
  • Strong understanding of Swedish Nasdaq rules, Swedish Companies Registration Office (Bolagsverket) rules, Market Abuse Regulation (MAR), Anti-Money Laundering (AML), General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), capital raising, and M&A transactions.
  • Expertise in regulatory compliance, corporate governance, gaming license application, litigation, and commercial law.
  • Skills: Exceptional leadership and stakeholder management skills.
  • Proven ability to navigate complex regulatory environments.
  • Strong negotiation, drafting, problem-solving, and dispute resolution skills.
  • Demonstrated experience managing capital raising and corporate transactions.


If you are excited about working in the fast-paced iGaming industry for this exciting fast growing company, and have the required experience, please apply today

companyDescription:
Sparbanken Eken är en nytänkande bank med en stark strävan att skapa värde för våra kunder och regionen där vi verkar. Vi erbjuder allt en storbank kan erbjuda, men med lokalt fokus. Vi har ca 70 medarbetare och kontor i Ryd, Delary, Grimslöv, Åryd, Älmeboda och Långasjö. Huvudkontoret finns i Ryd. 

Att vi är lokalt ägda, har små enheter/kontor och gillar att ge tillbaka till närsamhället skapar stolthet, både internt och hos våra kunder.
companySlogan: Världen är din, hos oss.
descriptionHtml:
Bli en del av Sparbanken Eken som AML-utredare!

Är du passionerad över att skydda våra finansiella system mot penningtvätt och finansiering av terrorism? Då har vi den perfekta möjligheten för dig inom Sparbanken Ekens säkerhetsavdelning. I denna tjänst kommer du att spela en nyckelroll i att skydda samhället från finansiell brottslighet och engagera dig i varierande och spännande uppgifter inom säkerhet och IT. Du får möjlighet att förbättra dina färdigheter inom ett område som är kritiskt både för banken och samhället.


Vem söker vi?

Vi söker dig med intresse av penningtvättsbekämpning (AML) och motverkande av terrorismfinansiering. Meriterande är erfarenhet av AML-arbete samt god kännedom om regelverk och branschtrender. Vi ser gärna att du har en examen inom juridik eller ekonomi, alternativt motsvarande erfarenhet.

Vi ser mycket positivt på om du har intresse och erfarenhet inom systemutveckling och digitalisering då dessa färdigheter blir allt viktigare för effektiv utredning och övrig IT-utveckling inom banken.


Om tjänsten

I denna position kommer du att utreda misstänkta aktiviteter relaterade till AML från olika källor, samt övervaka transaktioner och dokumentera varningar enligt fastställda protokoll. Du ansvarar för noggranna kundutredningar inklusive hantering av korrespondens, och fungerar som rådgivare till kollegor i frågor gällande AML. Snabba svar på myndighetsförfrågningar med noggrannhet är avgörande, liksom att förbereda rapporter till myndigheter såsom Finanspolisen.

För att lyckas hos oss behöver du hålla dig informerad genom utbildningar om utvecklande regelverk och metoder, samt driva förbättringar inom våra AML-processer för ökad effektivitet.

Vi ser att personlig lämplighet är avgörande därför genomförs bakgrundskontroller vid anställning och periodiskt därefter enligt policykrav.


Vad vi erbjuder dig

Hos oss är varje medarbetare viktig. Vi tror att du växer som mest när du får förtroende, möjlighet att påverka och ett tydligt syfte i ditt arbete. På Sparbanken Eken får du allt detta – i en framtidsinriktad fullservicebank med stark lokal närvaro och höga ambitioner. Här är du med och driver utvecklingen för både våra kunder och för den region vi verkar i.


Därför erbjuder vi dig:

  • Ett meningsfullt uppdrag – du är med och förverkligar idéer och drömmar för människor och företag i vår region.
  • En hållbar arbetsmiljö – ett generöst friskvårdsbidrag, regelbundna hälsokontroller och massage på arbetstid.
  • Trygghet och förmåner – du erbjuds sjukvårdsförsäkring, förmånliga villkor på bankens produkter och tjänster samt vår resultatbaserade bonus "Guldeken".
  • Möjlighet att påverka – vi är en platt organisation där idéer välkomnas och beslut tas nära verksamheten.
  • Ett samhällsengagemang att vara stolt över – sedan starten 2008 har vi delat ut över 50 miljoner kronor till lokala projekt inom utbildning, kultur och föreningsliv.


Ansökan

  • Tjänsten är tillsvidare och på 100 %.
  • Tillträde enligt överenskommelse.
  • Placeringsort efter individuell överenskommelse på något av våra bankkontor.
  • Ansökningar hanteras av vår HR-ansvarig Cecilia Turnstedt, hr@sparbankeneken.se, telefon 072-235 41 29
  • Frågor om tjänsten besvaras av Mats Clarin, Säkerhetschef telefon 073 – 827 28 03.
  • Vid önskad kontakt med facklig kontaktperson hör du av dig till HR-ansvarig Cecilia Turnstedt som hänvisar dig vidare till rätt person.


Sista ansökningsdag är den 7 september. Vi kommer dock att genomföra urval och intervjuer löpande, så tveka inte att skicka in din ansökan så snart som möjligt.


Ansök genom att skicka in CV och personligt brev till: hr@sparbankeneken.se


Läs mer om hur det är att jobba hos oss.

Välkommen med din ansökan!

companyDescription:
Rättvist byggande är en ideell förening öppen för alla långsiktiga byggherrar som på riktigt vill bidra till sund konkurrens och brottsfria byggen. 

Rättvist byggande är också vår arbetsmodell där byggherrar samverkar operativt och på så sätt skyddar varandra från risker kopplat till kriminalitet och arbetsrättsliga brister i sina byggprojekt.

Samverkan sker genom att byggherrarna bildat en ideell förening som utvecklat metoder och system som används i byggprojekten. Det ger medlemmarna större förmåga att upptäcka, motverka och förebygga riskerna än vad man kan skapa själva. Läs mer på vår hemsida.
companySlogan: Byggherrar i samverkan för sund konkurrens och brottsfria byggen
descriptionHtml:
Rättvist byggande söker utredande Compliance officer

Vi arbetar för en byggbransch fri från arbetslivskriminalitet. Vi är en idéburen verksamhet utan vinstintresse som bildats som ett strategiskt och operativt verktyg mellan stora, målmedvetna beställare av byggprojekt. Medlemmarna hos har både skyldigheter och rättigheter. Tillsammans utgör vi en av de största inköpsvolymerna av byggarbeten i Sverige. Vi har satt standarden för hur byggbranschen arbetar mot fusk av olika slag på byggarbetsplatser och vi har fått en tongivande roll inom socialt hållbart arbete i branschen. Vårt arbete går ut på att vi är experter på att upptäcka, motverka och förebygga fusk och social dumping genom industriella och affärsmässiga metoder. Vår hävstång är våra medlemmars beställarmakt i kombination med våra medarbetares kompetens och förmåga.

Vi vill ha modiga och ödmjuka medarbetare som är passionerade för vår vision. Våra medarbetare ska ha en egen inre vilja att uppnå höga resultat och disciplinen att lyckas med det. Alla våra medarbetar ska ha potential att bli bäst i Sverige på sina respektive ansvarsområden.”

Vi söker nu en utredande Compliance officer med engagemang för samhället och för rättvisa villkor

Vad är det ens för ett ord? Compliance officer? Nja, titlar är egentligen oviktiga för oss.

Vi söker en person som vill använda sin skarpa hjärna med hjärtat som kompass. Arbetet som utredande Compliance officer handlar om att planera, utreda, analysera och rapportera. Allt ifrån koll på arbetsplatsbesök ute på byggen till att granska avtal och identifiera avvikelser utifrån information du får in från vår fältverksamhet. Du stödjer kolleger med skarpa rapporter som ska användas vid avtalstvister och du kan rapportera statistik för våra medlemma. Du har också en roll att specialutreda vissa företag.

I arbetet ingår flera kontakter internt och vissa kontakter med medlemmar. Du förväntas bidra till vår egen utveckling som organisation med dina kunskaper och lärdomar.

Du ingår i ett team av experter med passion för vår vision. I arbetet krävs det god förmåga att både följa beslutade processer och delta i att utveckla dem.

Egenskaper för att trivas och lyckas hos oss är:

  • Värderingsstyrd: Genuin, inre vilja att skapa rättvisa villkor för företag och människor
  • Koordinerande: Tycka om att ha koll och olika rapporter pågående samtidigt
  • Analytisk och noggrann: Fallenhet för mönster och avvikelser och noga med faktadetaljer
  • Vi vill gärna ha en samlande kraft och en relationsbyggande kollega i gruppen

Kompetenser som vi tror är värdefulla att ha med sig:

  • Vana att arbeta med byggentreprenader och byggavtal
  • Förmåga att kunna kommunicera på formellt och korrekt skriftspråk på svenska
  • Erfarenhet från arbete i eller nära offentlig verksamhet
  • Erfarenhet av en uppföljande roll såsom revision, controlling eller leverantörsuppföljning

Vi märker att vi är bäst när vi träffas regelbundet, därför är arbetet förlagt på plats men möjlighet finns att arbeta hemifrån någon gång då och då.

Placering: vårt kontor i Stockholm

Omfattning: Heltid

Anställningsform: Tillsvidare

Om du är intresserad efter att ha läst ovanstående så tar vi gärna emot din ansökan (cv och ett kort brev per e-post). I ansökan behöver du svara på dessa frågor.

  • Varför vill du arbeta för Rättvist byggande?
  • Vad är det hos dig som gör att du skulle passa i denna yrkesroll?
  • Vad innebär ”rättvisa” för dig (kopplat till vårt arbete)?
  • Godkänner du att vi genomför en bakgrundskontroll på dig?

Det kan tillkomma ett enklare arbetsprov under rekryteringsprocessen.

Ansökningsdatum:

Just nu har vi en öppen ansökan och tar intervjuer löpande. Vi kommer inte att intervjua under sommaren men läser ansökningarna som kommer in.

companyDescription:
Norion Bank Group (formerly Collector Bank) is a business-oriented Nordic financing bank. The Group’s brands – Norion Bank, Walley and Collector – offer customized financing solutions that meet distinct needs in three customer segments: medium-sized corporates and real estate companies, merchants, and private individuals. As a specialist in financing solutions, Norion Bank Group is a leading complement to traditional large banks, with a vision of being the leading Nordic financing bank in its chosen segments.  
   
Norion Bank offers corporate and real estate loans, as well as factoring for medium-sized corporates and real estate companies. The Walley brand offers flexible payment and checkout solutions to merchants and private individuals. The Collector brand offers personal loans and credit cards to private customers, as well as savings accounts to private individuals and companies. Norion Bank Group was founded in 1999 and has offices in Gothenburg, Stockholm, Helsingborg, Oslo and Helsinki. Business is conducted through Norion Bank AB (public), which is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm.
descriptionHtml:
Vill du ta en central roll i en snabbfotad och växande finansieringsbank? Nu söker vi en specialist med djup kunskap inom AML/CTF och intresse för modellriskhantering och IT-kravställning.

Som AML System & Process Specialist får du arbeta affärsnära och driva utvecklingen av bankens modellriskhantering inom AML/CTF. Rollen är bred och självständig med stort ansvar i en organisation som kombinerar entreprenöriell drivkraft med tydlig struktur och långsiktig ägarbild.

OM ROLLEN

Denna centrala roll innebär att driva och hantera bankens modellriskarbete avseende AML/CTF. Du ansvarar därtill för att löpande kontrollera och följa upp datakvalitet ur ett AML/CTF perspektiv samt att säkerställa att banken har en tillfredställande kontrollmiljö vad gäller modellrisk såväl som datakvalitet. Du kommer att arbeta i gränslandet mellan AML, affär och IT, där du har en avgörande roll i utvecklingen av bolagets hantering av AML/CTF modeller. Teamet består idag av ett tiotal medarbetare, alla under ledning av avdelningens Head of AML and Sanctions, som även du kommer att rapportera till. Din geografiska placering kan vara på vårt kontor i antingen Göteborg eller Stockholm.

Arbetsuppgifterna innefattar bland annat att:


  • Ansvara för bankens AML/CTF-modeller och tillhörande system, inklusive bland annat transaktionsövervakning och riskklassificering. I uppdraget ingår även utveckling, förvaltning, testning, dokumentation samt löpande uppföljning av relevanta modeller och system, i syfte att säkerställa att de är ändamålsenliga och täcker de risker som identifieras i bankens allmänna riskbedömning.
  • Säkerställa korrekt och ändamålsenlig datakvalitet i AML/CTF-modeller och analyser, genom övervakning av datakällor, identifiering av brister samt initiering av förbättringsåtgärder.
  • Upprätta test- och modelldokumentation samt löpande rapporter och beslutsunderlag.
  • Ta fram och analysera olika typer av kund- och transaktionsdata.
  • Kravställa IT-utveckling och följa upp implementering.
  • Driva förändringsarbete och leda AML-relaterade projekt, med ett helhetsansvar från behovsanalys till genomförande, ofta i samarbete med flera interna parter.
  • Ansvara för att i nära samarbete med affären, kravställa gentemot IT avseende AML-relaterad utveckling.
  • Bidra till det strategiska AML/CTF-arbetet och bygga starka samarbeten med nyckelfunktioner inom banken.
  • Inneha egna ansvarsområden inom AML Office och självständigt driva och genomföra arbetet inom dessa.


OM DIG

En lyckad kandidat för tjänsten har en bred bakgrund inom AML, med erfarenhet av modellriskhantering, processer och kravställning. Du bör ha arbetat med IT-kravställning, analys av data och har god vana av valideringsprocesser samt ha en relevant akademisk examen inom ekonomi, IT eller motsvarande och minst 4 års erfarenhet av liknande arbetsuppgifter, gärna från en dynamisk miljö som exempelvis fintech, nischbank eller konsultverksamhet. Extra meriterande är om du tidigare haft en projektledarroll, gärna inom IT-utveckling.

Du bör ha ett bra öga för affären, vara en naturlig lagspelare och ha ett lösningsorienterat och pragmatiskt synsätt. Som person är du mycket strukturerad och självgående, med en god analytisk förmåga och hög integritet. Du har mycket god förmåga att utrycka dig i skrift och är van att upprätta underlag av hög kvalitet. Du behärskar excel, powerpoint och analysverktyg eller motsvarande verktyg väl.

AFFÄRSOMRÅDET NORION BANK

Norion Bank är en affärsnära nordisk finansieringsbank, som är specialister på finansieringslösningar för medelstora företag och fastighetsbolag. Norion Bank erbjuder företags- och fastighetskrediter samt factoring och agerar som en stabil partner till bolag som vill ta nästa utvecklingssteg. Norion Bank finns på den svenska, norska, finska och tyska marknaden.

OM NORION BANK GROUP

Norion Bank Group är en affärsnära nordisk finansieringsbank. Genom koncernens varumärken Norion Bank, Walley och Collector erbjuds kundanpassade finansieringstjänster som möter distinkta kundbehov inom tre kundsegment: medelstora företag och fastighetsbolag, handlare och privatpersoner. Som specialist på finansieringslösningar är Norion Bank Group ett ledande komplement till traditionella storbanker, med visionen att vara den främsta nordiska finansieringsbanken inom de utvalda segmenten.  

Norion Banks erbjudande omfattar företags- och fastighetskrediter samt factoring för medelstora företag och fastighetsbolag. Genom varumärket Walley erbjuds flexibla betal- och checkoutlösningar till handlare och privatpersoner. Varumärket Collector tillhandahåller privatlån och kreditkort till privatpersoner samt sparkonton för privatpersoner och företag. Norion Bank Group (tidigare Collector Bank) grundades 1999 och har kontor i Göteborg, Stockholm, Helsingborg, Oslo och Helsingfors. Verksamheten bedrivs genom Norion Bank AB (publ) som är noterat på Nasdaq Stockholm.  

NORION BANKS ERBJUDANDE TILL DIG

För oss är det självklart att varje individs utveckling värdesätts högt vilket innebär ett stort fokus på självledarskap och kompetensutveckling. Gemenskap, innovation och modern arbetsmetodik är saker som kännetecknar Norion Bank Group. Vi erbjuder dig helt enkelt en organisation med snabba beslutsvägar, stor öppenhet och mycket energi.

Våra värdeord Affärsmässighet, Engagemang och Omtanke genomsyrar allt vi gör och som medarbetare hos oss blir du en del av vår starka företagskultur. Vi erbjuder dig ett spännande bolag med goda utvecklingsmöjligheter, löpande kompetensutveckling och gemensamma aktiviteter på och utanför arbetsplatsen.

Vi undanber oss kontakt med rekryteringsbolag, konsultbolag samt annonsörer i rekryteringen.

INTRESSERAD?

Kul! Urval och intervjuer sker löpande så ansök redan idag!

Frågor? Välkommen att höra av dig till oss!

För frågor kring teamet och rollen, kontakta gärna Ester Sandgren, Head of AML and Sanctions på ester.sandgren@norionbank.se

För frågor om oss som arbetsgivare eller rekryteringsprocessen, kontakta gärna Celie Weinhöfer, HR Business Partner på celie.weinhofer@norionbank.se

Norion Bank Group är en affärsnära nordisk finansieringsbank. Genom koncernens varumärken Norion Bank, Walley och Collector erbjuds kundanpassade finansieringstjänster som möter distinkta kundbehov inom tre kundsegment: medelstora företag och fastighetsbolag, handlare och privatpersoner. Som specialist på finansieringslösningar är Norion Bank Group ett ledande komplement till traditionella storbanker, med visionen att vara den främsta nordiska finansieringsbanken inom de utvalda segmenten.

 

Norion Banks erbjudande omfattar företags- och fastighetskrediter samt factoring för medelstora företag och fastighetsbolag. Genom varumärket Walley erbjuds flexibla betal- och checkoutlösningar till handlare och privatpersoner. Varumärket Collector tillhandahåller privatlån och kreditkort till privatpersoner samt sparkonton för privatpersoner och företag. Norion Bank (tidigare Collector Bank) grundades 1999 och har kontor i Göteborg, Stockholm, Helsingborg, Oslo och Helsingfors. companyDescription:
The future of mobile isn’t on the horizon, it’s happening now. At Ericsson, we’re building the foundation for an open network ecosystem where industries, developers, and enterprises thrive.

The convergence of 5G, AI, cloud, and network APIs isn’t just a technological shift; it’s a transformation that is redefining industries and enhancing everyday life. Open, programmable networks are enabling real-time innovation and unlocking new business models across the globe.

Imagine a world where developers can dynamically access network capabilities on demand, where enterprises don’t just use connectivity but shape it. This isn’t a distant vision, it’s the ecosystem we’re creating today.

Collaboration fuels everything we do. By working across industries, we’re designing a future where connectivity isn’t just seamless. It’s intelligent, programmable, and transformative.

The shift is happening. Are you part of it?
companySlogan: We’re leading the next wave of mobile with 5G, AI, cloud, and network APIs, creating an open network ecosystem.
descriptionHtml:
About This Opportunity

Ericsson is looking for an accomplished and positive team member to strengthen its Group Data Protection Office. The critical role of the Office is to carry out thestatutory tasks under Article 39 GDPR for the parent company and main operational company of the Ericsson Group, as well as coordinating the reportingactivities of the Group’s community of local DPOs and Privacy Managers and assessing and reporting levels of compliance within the Group to the audit andcompliance committee of the Board of Directors.

What You Will

  • Make a positive contribution to a highly qualified team of experts carrying out tasks delegated by the Group DPO within an independent assurance functionhosted within Ericsson's Group Legal Affairs.
  • Have the confidence and ability to step up as a deputy DPO whenever needed.
  • Communicate effectively and set up positive working relationships with your team members and key stakeholders at all levels within the organization.
  • Deliver independent, courageous, fact-based recommendations and opinions.
  • Act as an impartial facilitator between the business, supervisory authorities, and data subjects.
  • Maintain and develop your expertise and professional skills, actively advocating for data protection rights within all areas of the business.

You will bring

  • A law degree, in addition to at least one Data Protection and/or Privacy certification such as, CIPP/E, CIPM, with a proven record of expertise and experiencein the GDPR, Swedish and or other data protection laws.
  • A minimum 7 years’ continuous professional experience in data protection compliance. including at least 4 years of recent experience as a DPO.
  • Solid experience working in a global matrix organization, preferably within the ICT sector or other regulated industry.
  • A sound, demonstrable record of leading and running projects, whilst also able to work independently within time limits.
  • Proficiency in English is required. The preferred candidate will also be able to communicate well in Swedish.
  • Resilience, perseverance, and the ability to maintain a balanced perspective.
  • A high level of integrity, and well-developed communication skills are critical to this role!
  • The successful candidate should be independent, courageous, stress tolerant and able to remain calm under pressure, with their sense of humour intact!

Additional information:

Primary location for this role: Stockholm, Sweden. Vetting including references and relevant background screening will be conducted for final candidates. Last date to apply is 15th of sept.

What happens once you apply?

Click Here to find all you need to know about what our typical hiring process looks like.

We encourage you to consider applying to jobs where you might not meet all the criteria. We recognize that we all have transferrable skills, and we can support you with the skills that you need to develop.

Encouraging a diverse and inclusive organization is core to our values at Ericsson, that's why we champion it in everything we do. We truly believe that by collaborating with people with different experiences we drive innovation, which is essential for our future growth. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and realize their full potential as part of our Ericsson team. Ericsson is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. learn more.

Primary country and city: Sweden (SE) || Stockholm

Job details: Compliance Officer

Job Stage: Job Stage 8

Primary Recruiter: Menna Belacevic

Hiring Manager: Paul Graham

companyDescription:
Cidestra is a Management Consulting firm focusing on Strategy and Transformation within the Financial Services sector. Cidestra is part of the Newground Alliance - an alliance of independent specialist consulting firms.

We provide deep insights into the most critical disruptive forces within Financial Services: Emerging Technologies, Regulatory Pressure and Changing Customer Behaviour. By combining these insights with industry expertise and strong skills within innovation, design and transformation, we help our clients to be in the forefront of the market development.

We have advised and supported the leading financial institutions and Fintechs in the Nordics and in Europe in a wide range of initiatives, ranging from Strategy to Design and Implementation.
companySlogan: Cidestra is a Management Consulting firm focusing on Strategy and Transformation within the Financial Services Sector.
descriptionHtml:
Cidestra is seeking a dynamic management consultant within Financial Crime Prevention to join our team. In this role, you will assist our clients within Banking, Insurance and Fintech to reshape their operations within Financial Crime Prevention.

This is an exciting opportunity for a proactive, results- and development driven professional who thrives in a fast-paced, client-focused, dynamic learning environment and wants to work within the first line of defense. You will play a crucial role in shaping and implementing solutions for client challenges, particularly within Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Financial Crime Prevention.

Key Responsibilities

  • Collaborate with clients to understand their business needs and challenges
  • Design and implementing Financial Crime prevention frameworks and components – incl. KYC, screening, risk assessments, risk models, monitoring, reporting
  • Build strong client relationships and ensure exceptional service delivery
  • Communicate solutions & change needs and manage key stakeholders
  • Lead change streams and initiatives and coordinate with cross-functional departments

Key Requirements

  • 4-13 years of experience of change initiatives within Financial Crime Prevention – in management consulting OR within Banking and Finance sector coupled with management consulting experience
  • Proven background in business analysis, project management and/or management of agile development coupled with good analytical skills and a problem-solving mindset
  • Experience in business design and transformation
  • Subject-matter knowledge and experience from the financial services industry, especially within AML / Financial Crime Prevention
  • Experience working within the first line of defense
  • Passion for innovation and disruption
  • Good communication skills - fluency in Swedish and English

What We Can Offer

To be part of an ambitious, forward-thinking company with a flat hierarchal structure giving you direct access to the partners and senior colleagues

To work in an environment based upon trust, where we collaborate and everyone is seen as a valued member of a tight team. We believe that coaching, learning, collaboration, well-being, having fun and feeling psychological safety is the foundation for a high performing team!

To be engaged on some of the most interesting and complex projects in the Financial Services sector, especially in the areas of payments, open finance, financial crime prevention and sustainability.

Personal development and career progression based upon your own individual merit and initiatives, which is not ruled by a hierarchal culture or process.

Cidestra offers a competitive compensation model creating great opportunity for successful candidates.

To be part of Newground Alliance, an alliance of twelve companies, that employ 260 consultants giving you the opportunity of being involved in a larger ecosystem of cultures and opportunities.

#Cidestra

Cidestra is a management consulting firm focusing on strategy and transformation within the Financial Services industry.

We have advised the leading financial institutions in the Nordics and in Europe in a wide range of initiatives, ranging from Strategy to Design and Implementation.

Our company is part of the Newground Alliance - an alliance of independent niche consulting firms with various areas of expertise. The group has in total over 250 employees with office at Norrmalmstorg 14 in Stockholm.

companyDescription: Ikano Bank creates possibilities for better living by offering simple, fair, and affordable services, enabling a healthy economy for the many people and businesses. Its offer includes savings and loan products for consumers, sales support services for retailers, and leasing and factoring solutions for businesses. Ikano Bank operates in Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway, the UK, Poland, Germany and Austria, and is a part of Ingka Group. Ikano Bank’s head office is located in Malmö, Sweden and the company is registered in Älmhult, Sweden where the business was once founded..
companySlogan: Together, we create a great place to work where people are inspired to be the best they can be.
descriptionHtml:
About the role

As the B2B Fraud Excellence Specialist at Ikano Bank, you will play a crucial role in protecting the bank and its customers from fraud. You will be the organization’s subject matter expert in B2B fraud, with a key focus on the Swedish market.


Your daily work will involve both preventive actions and fraud investigations, ranging from simpler cases to more complex, high-risk matters. You will drive and implement preventive measures, ensuring consistent practices across the Nordic markets, and take ownership of regular reporting and analysis to monitor fraud trends and effectiveness of controls. You will collaborate closely with internal stakeholders in Commercial, Credit, AML & KYC as well as Collection to deliver a comprehensive fraud risk strategy with a commercial mindset. Maintaining a proactive and curious mindset, you will identify new tools and innovative methods to enhance the bank’s fraud prevention and detection capabilities, improving efficiency and effectiveness.


In This Role, Your Key Responsibilities Will Include

  • Managing daily fraud prevention activities and fraud investigations, ranging from minor to complex cases, with a focus on the Swedish market.
  • Developing and implementing new and effective controls across our Nordic markets to continuously strengthen the mitigation of fraud risks in B2B Leasing and Factoring.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally and across markets to ensure a holistic approach to fraud issues.
  • Providing training and guidance to colleagues and external partners on fraud-related matters.
  • Participate in and represent Fraud in various internal and external forums.
  • Keeping up-to-date with emerging fraud trends and threats, adapting strategies as needed to ensure the fraud risk remains within the bank’s risk appetite.

The role is based in Sundbyberg, Stockholm.


The last application date is September 14th.


About You

We are looking for a candidate with strong analytical skills and proven experience in one or more of the following areas: fraud prevention, late collections, or credit risk. Previous experience within the B2B leasing and/or factoring industry is considered a strong advantage.


You have an academic background in Business Administration, Criminology, or Law — or possess at least five years of equivalent and relevant professional experience.


You Bring

  • Proven experience in leading initiatives across markets.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
  • Fluency in Swedish and professional proficiency in English.


We Also See You As Someone Who

  • Is self-driven, proactive, and takes ownership.
  • Thrives in dynamic environments and manages multiple priorities effectively.
  • Has strong customer focus and understands the business context of fraud and/or credit risks.
  • Demonstrates excellent problem-solving and investigative skills.
  • Communicates clearly and confidently, both verbally and in writing.
  • Works collaboratively across functions and organizational boundaries.


We will place great emphasis on personal suitability, as well as your ability to work both independently and collaboratively across teams.

About Ikano Bank

Ikano Bank creates possibilities for better living by offering simple, fair, and affordable services, enabling a healthy economy for the many people and businesses. Its offer includes savings and loan products for consumers, sales support services for retailers, and leasing and factoring solutions for businesses. Ikano Bank operates in Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway, the UK, Poland, Germany and Austria, and is a part of Ingka Group. Ikano Bank’s head office is located in Malmö, Sweden and the company is registered in Älmhult, Sweden where the business was once founded.


Contact information

In this recruitment, Ikano Bank collaborates with Adecco.

If you have any questions about the role or the recruitment process, you are welcome to contact the responsible recruiter:

Marie Ekvall at marie.ekvall@adecco.se


We look forward to receiving your application!

descriptionHtml:
As our Compliance Officer, you will be a member of a fantastic global team of 10 Compliance Officers, and report to the Chief Compliance Officer.

  • Do you want to use your competencies in an international environment?
  • Do you enjoy combining independent monitoring with being pro-active and giving advice?
  • Do you like working in a high pace environment with great opportunities to influence the Compliance area of a Bank?

Then we are looking for you!

You will be part of Ikano Bank, where we create possibilities for a better living by providing banking and financial services. We do it with different perspectives, natural curiosity and long-term focus. A unique chance for greater responsibility and future opportunities. Let’s achieve great things together.

Help us simplify finance for the many people

The 2nd line Compliance Function is a vital independent function. The function’s role is to, with a risk-based approach, identify compliance gaps, recommend actions and support the Bank by giving advice and support. Play an active role in the maintenance and continuous improvement of the Bank’s compliance framework.

You will be based in Sweden, but your work arena will be all Ikano Bank’s branches, from your location preferably in Malmö.

Main Tasks As Compliance Officer

  • Identify, analyze and interpret and produce documentation on new external regulations that are relevant for the Bank’s licensed operation.
  • Independently assess compliance risks and thus contributing to the Compliance Function's overall risk assessment.
  • Perform monitoring and controls to secure the Bank’s adherence to external regulations. Examples of areas are Consumer Finance related regulations such as payment regulations, complaints, License Management, authority reporting, Customer protection and financial innovation regulations.
  • Produce reports based on the monitoring and control including recommendations based on the observations.
  • Provide advice, support and training on external requirements applicable to the Bank's licensed operations and ensure that governing documents within relevant areas are kept up to date.
  • Inform and train co-workers on new or changed external requirements and relevant internal regulations.
  • Regular written reporting to relevant stakeholders, including input to CEO and Board reporting.
  • Represent the Compliance Function on relevant meetings and committees.
  • Actively support the business in their work with compliance risk management and promote a strong Compliance culture
  • We are looking for a Compliance officer with a broad banking knowledge of regulations connected to our licensed operation. The areas AML and GDPR are already well covered in our team by your future colleagues.

We offer you

Operating at the center of events, you will have a very hands-on and active role, and create a great foundation for further development. You will get the chance to push boundaries and influence your daily work. It is essential that you enjoy working within a broad range of Compliance areas and countries and that you have a pro-active and “can-do”-approach, whilst maintaining an independent position. All as part of a caring and open culture where we live our values, work hard, have fun and operate with a long-term perspective.

Are you the one for Ikano?

  • You have a law degree (LL.M.) or equivalent
  • At least 5 years of relevant work experience from the financial sector within the Compliance area.
  • You have proven experience in interpreting external regulations applicable to a Swedish Bank.
  • You have the ability to produce professional written Compliance reports.
  • You are fluent in English and either Swedish, Danish or Norwegian.
  • You have an excellent ability to communicate and interact with people at all levels in the organization.
  • You have high integrity and are familiar with the 2nd line requirements.
  • You like to work independently, and you are proactive with an attitude to inspire and motivate people. It is essential that you are flexible end enjoy shifting between countries / areas / tasks.

Join us now – together we will find a better way

We will evaluate candidates and hold interviews on a continuous basis, last date to apply is September 21st.

Ikano Bank’s head office is located in Malmö, Sweden and the company is registered in Älmhult, Sweden where the business was once founded.

companyDescription:
We help our clients and colleagues grow — and our communities thrive — by protecting and promoting Possibility. We seek better ways to manage risk and define more effective paths to the right outcome. We go beyond risk to rewards for our clients, our company, our colleagues, and the communities in which we serve.

Marsh, a business of Marsh McLennan﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿ (NYSE: MMC), is the world’s top insurance broker and risk advisor. Marsh McLennan is ﻿﻿﻿﻿a global leader in risk, strategy and people, advising clients in 130 countries across four businesses: Marsh, Guy Carpenter, Mercer﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿ and Oliver Wyman﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿. With annual revenue of $23 billion and more than 85,000 colleagues, Marsh McLennan helps build the confidence to thrive through the power of perspective.
companySlogan: We shape the future through our perspective, expertise and solutions, empowering our clients to thrive.
descriptionHtml:
We are seeking a talented individual to join our Governance, Risk & Compliance team here at Marsh McLennan. This role will be based in Stockholm. This is a hybrid role that has a requirement of working at least three - four days per week in the office.

The GRC (Governance/Risk/Compliance) function at Marsh Management Services Sweden consists of a team of consultants who work together to help Swedish and English-speaking captive owners and insurance company clients manage GRC according to the Solvency 2 regulations. The member will work closely with the team and clients, learning and taking on more responsibilities over time, including participation in various projects.

Please note: if applying please ensure that your CV & application is in English.

The role: Junior Governance, Risk & Compliance Analyst

To thrive in this role, the ideal candidate should be humble, willing to take on any task, and be self-driven and proactive. The main responsibilities include assist in conducting and following up on regulatory compliance/risk reviews, coordinating board meetings, and supporting ongoing client projects and reporting.

We will count on you to:

  • Assist in the development of the annual reviews based on risk assessments.
  • Assist in conducting internal audits, risk and compliance reviews in line with the plans.
  • Participate in identifying and evaluating risks associated with business processes and operations.
  • Assist in preparing clear and concise review reports detailing findings, risks, and recommendations for improvement.
  • Present review findings to internal management and assist in developing action plans to address identified issues.
  • Recommend enhancements to current review methodologies and tools to ensure continuous improvement in the way we work.
  • Work closely with other departments, including operations, finance and accounting, to foster a culture of risk awareness and compliance.
  • Participate in cross-functional teams to support company initiatives such as onboarding of new clients as well as corporate governance matters.
  • Keep updated with and document relevant regulatory and risk developments

What you need to have:

  • Recently graduated (within the last 1-2 years) with a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business, Economics, Management, Law, or another relevant discipline.
  • Fluent in Swedish and a competent English speaker, with excellent verbal and written communication skills in both languages.
  • Strong business acumen, problem-solving skills, and a genuine interest in the insurance industry.
  • Ambitious, proactive, and eager to learn, with a positive attitude and the ability to thrive in a high-paced environment.
  • Based in or near Stockholm, with a flexible approach to work arrangements.

What makes you stand out:

  • Interest in working within a large, global organization that values diversity and professional growth.
  • Personality and attitude that demonstrate a willingness to learn and develop in a supportive, team-oriented environment.
  • Work experience of 1-2 years in a similar role in the financial services industry.

Why join our team:

  • We help you be your best through professional development opportunities, interesting work and supportive leaders.
  • We foster a vibrant and inclusive culture where you can work with talented colleagues to create new solutions and have impact for colleagues and clients.
  • Our scale enables us to provide a range of career opportunities, as well as benefits and rewards to enhance your well-being.

descriptionHtml:
At Resurs, we work every day to protect our customers and the integrity of the financial system. Now we are looking for a Financial Crime Prevention Specialist to join our team and help us stay one step ahead when it comes to AML and counter-terrorist financing. If you are motivated by structure and impact, this could be the right challenge for you.

Get an idea of the role

As a Financial Crime Prevention Specialist, you will play a key role in ensuring that the bank complies with AML and CTF regulations.

Your responsibilities will include:

  • Reviewing and analysing transactions to detect suspicious activity
  • Performing KYC, ODD, and EDD for new and existing customers
  • Investigating AML alerts and reporting suspicious activity when needed
  • Supporting and training colleagues in AML/CTF-related matters
  • Contributing to the improvement of internal processes and routines
  • Staying up to date with relevant laws and regulatory changes


On a personal level

To succeed in this role, you are someone who combines deep regulatory knowledge with strong analytical skills and sound judgment. You are structured, thorough and solution-oriented, and take pride in doing things the right way.

We believe you bring:

  • 5+ years of experience in AML, compliance or financial risk management
  • Strong knowledge of KYC/ODD/EDD processes and AML/CTF regulation
  • Experience from the financial sector, preferably within card services, banking or fraud management
  • A collaborative mindset and confidence to communicate clearly across teams
  • A degree in law, finance or another relevant field
  • Full professional fluency in both Swedish and English


Resurs in brief

At Resurs, we make everyday finances easier through innovative and customer-focused solutions. With customers across the Nordics and more than 800 colleagues, we are committed to creating long-term value for individuals, businesses, and society. Recognized as a career company for the past six years, we put strong emphasis on both personal and professional growth. We want to make a difference by striving to be our best, acting with kindness, and building trust through clarity and collaboration. Our heart beats strong and it shows in everything we do.

Although we are based in the Nordics, we are enriched by the breadth of perspectives brought by colleagues from different countries. We foster a welcoming and supportive culture, where collaboration drives us to achieve the best outcomes for our users.

Apply today!

Don’t wait to send in your application. Selection and interviews take place on an ongoing basis, and the position may be filled before the last application date.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Important information

On final candidates, we carry out a detailed background check via an external company.

  • In this recruitment process, the supplier is solely responsible for the personal data they collect and process. Resurs is solely responsible for the personal data processed by us in the recruitment process.


Resurs has almost 50 years of experience in retail finance and is one of the leading niche banks in the Nordics. We help companies and private individuals with lending, savings and payments. Today, we have a customer base of slightly more than 6 million customers and around 800 employees in the Nordic region.

Follow us on Instagram:

descriptionHtml:
Get an idea of the role

Are you ready to take on a key role where you will take lead on the ICT risk oversight within the Bank? We are looking for someone to join our independent Risk function, where you will report directly to the Head of Risk and work alongside highly skilled professionals. Together with your colleagues, you will drive, develop and take ownership of the current risk frameworks.

Your primary focus will be to independently assess and analyze ICT risks, enhance existing frameworks, and ensure that the Bank’s risk management practices align with regulatory requirements, industry standards, and best practices. While your core responsibility lies within ICT risk, the role may at times extend into broader non-financial risk areas, requiring a flexible mindset and a holistic understanding of risk across the organization.

What will you do?

Some of your key responsibilities will include:

  • Support the development and enhancement of risk management practices, frameworks, and methodologies
  • Execute on the annual plan which includes audits, control activities, risk assessment and training
  • Ensure that the Bank's risk management practices are aligned with defined risk appetite and tolerance levels
  • Draft quarterly reports to senior management and the Board of Directors
  • Stay updated on regulatory changes impacting risk management and internal control
  • Advice and support internal stakeholders on risk related matters
  • Monitor, track and share knowledge on the risks Resurs is exposed to


Requirements

What is needed in this role:

  • Proficiency in Swedish and English, both written and spoken
  • A relevant university degree or equivalent
  • A sound knowledge of regulatory requirements relating to internal control and risk management. Knowledge of relevant SFSA's regulations and general guidelines, EBA ICT guidelines, DORA and relevant industry standards such as ISO 27001 and 22301
  • Analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Knowledge and experience from the financial sector
  • Previous experience in a second-line role or as an internal auditor is meriting
  • Skills on ICT risk management with relevant certifications such as CISSP, CISM, or CRISC are meriting


On a personal level

This is a hybrid role, with placement in either Helsingborg or Stockholm, offering flexibility while staying closely connected to our teams. We’re looking for a proactive individual with a positive, can-do attitude, someone who thrives in a collaborative environment and brings exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills. You take ownership, deliver high-quality results, and approach challenges with creativity and a solution-oriented mindset. Balancing attention to detail with a strategic perspective is key. In return, we offer extensive learning and development opportunities in a workplace that genuinely values work-life balance and personal growth.

Resurs in brief

At Resurs, we make everyday finances easier through innovative and customer-focused solutions. With customers across the Nordics and more than 800 colleagues, we are committed to creating long-term value for individuals, businesses, and society. Recognized as a career company for the past six years, we put strong emphasis on both personal and professional growth. We want to make a difference by striving to be our best, acting with kindness, and building trust through clarity and collaboration. Our heart beats strong and it shows in everything we do.

Join an international team

Although we are based in the Nordics, our team is enriched by the breadth of perspectives brought by colleagues from different countries. We foster a welcoming and supportive culture, where collaboration drives us to achieve the best outcomes for our users.

Apply today!

Don’t wait to send in your application. Selection and interviews take place on an ongoing basis, and the position may be filled before the last application date.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Important information

On final candidates, we carry out a detailed background check via an external company.

Today, we have a customer base of slightly more than 6 million customers and around 800 employees in the Nordic region.
