descriptionHtml:
Jobbannons

22 augusti 2025

Finance and Compliance Officer

Sista ansökningsdag: 12 september, 2025

Plats: Malmö

We are seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Finance and Compliance Officer to join our dynamic international programme team.

Barnfonden is a politically and religiously independent child rights organisation based in Malmö, Sweden. Since 1991, we’ve worked to improve the lives of children around the world through rights-based, climate-aware, and community-driven development. As a member of the ChildFund Alliance, we support programmes across Africa, Asia, and Latin America, working alongside local partners to help children thrive at every life stage. A core programme funded through the generous support of over 20,000 Swedish sponsors and donors is supplemented by grants from major institutions such as Forum Civ/Sida, Radiohjälpen and the EU.

We are currently seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Finance and Compliance Officer to join our dynamic international programme team. The role is vital in ensuring strong financial quality, accountability, and compliance throughout the project cycle in line with Barnfonden’s and our donors’ requirements.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in the development of new grant applications, with a focus on project and programme budgeting.
  • Ensure quality assurance and financial reporting for projects, including proper analysis and presentation of financial data.
  • Coordinate and support audit processes, financial follow-up, and reporting to donors, partners, and internal stakeholders.
  • Identify and monitor financial risks in collaboration with the Business and Project Controller.
  • Support programme team with fund transfers, fund tracking, reconciliations, and reporting for projects.
  • Review and ensure compliance with project agreements, budgets, and donor requirements.
  • Assist in Barnfonden work with budget prognosis, monthly close processes and reconciliations.
  • Contribute to desk audits and internal reviews of partners to strengthen financial accountability and transparency.
  • Collaborate across departments and support other finance-related activities as required.

Essential Requirements

  • Minimum 2 years of relevant experience in a similar role.
  • Experience in financial follow-up, budgeting, project audits, and reporting to institutional donors (e.g. Sida, EU, Forum Civ).
  • Experience in accounting and bookkeeping.
  • Strong communication skills in both Swedish and English, written and spoken.
  • Excellent analytical skills and high attention to detail.
  • Ability to interpret financial information and make informed recommendations.
  • Relevant academic degree or equivalent professional experience.

READ MORE AND APPLY HERE

descriptionHtml:
Cybersphere Limited is a leader in the field of data protection, offering innovative solutions based on advanced cryptographic methods and blockchain technology. Since 1999, we have been building corporate infrastructure with a focus on security, high automation, and real-world business cases. Our systems work in real time, are resistant to cyber attacks, and are ready to grow with you.

The Role

You Will Be Responsible For

  • Conducting penetration testing, simulating an attack on the system to find exploitable weaknesses.
  • Developing and implementing security framework, policies, processes/procedures and guidelines.
  • Maintaining security subject-matter expertise and keep abreast of best practices & trends.
  • Managing vulnerability assessment.
  • Oversight & resolution of security incidents.

Ideal Profile

  • You possess a Degree/Diploma in Computer Science, Engineering or related field.
  • You have at least 2 years experience, ideally within a IT Security role.
  • You have good interpersonal and communication skills and are adept at working with multiple stakeholders to drive desired outcomes.
  • You have working knowledge of Multi-sig, KYC/AML and ICO / Token Sales
  • You are a strong networker & relationship builder
  • You possess strong analytical skills and are comfortable dealing with numerical data
  • You are adaptable and thrive in changing environments

What's on Offer?

  • Work within a company with a solid track record of success
  • Work alongside & learn from best in class talent
  • Leadership Role

descriptionHtml:
Assignment description

For our client we are looking for Test Manager, Stockholm

Responsibilities:

  • Ensuring that the following activities are conducted in an efficient and well documented way, i.e. working according to latest version of company Test Strategy:
  • Test planning and control,
  • Test analysis and design,
  • Test implementation and execution,
  • Test evaluation: exit criteria and report,
  • Capability to co-ordinate and take responsibility for all QA activities
  • Driving continuous improvement and introduction of best practices, ensuring the availability of test environment and test data, understanding and addressing testing skill gaps as well as coaching and helping team members to ensure agility and excellent deliveries.
  • Implements one of our frameworks for API tests automation using Postman, Swagger, Robot Framework
  • Develops and maintains test automation scripts
  • Creates and maintains test cases
  • Provides Test documentation/evidence
  • Follows company common data model

Requirements:

  • SQL Concepts
  • ISTQB Testing Knowledge
  • Experience in managing software testing projects/activities and resources
  • Proactive approach towards communication, identifying challenges and solutions to problems, planning work for QA team
  • Formulate / review actions to ensure software testing strategies are aligned with company Quality and Testing Standards
  • Review business / software requirements and provide input.
  • Prepare / review test plans.
  • Analyze test results and test metrics.
  • Prepare / review / present test reports.
  • Coordinate with other managers (Project Manager, Development Manager, etc) to ensure the highest level of software quality.
  • Communicate with development teams, architects, test environment people to define E2E testing scope and test environments to be used for testing.
  • Experience in SAFe and QA in Agile
  • Hands-On Experience in test management tools (preferably qTest or similar)

Good to have:

  • It may be good to have experience also in Selenium.
  • Understanding of AML will be an advantage
  • Gen AI and ML experience will be an advantage

Required Skills: Software Testing, istqb, SQL Concepts,Test Analysis

Languages: English

Start Date: 08 Jan 2025

Assignment period: 1 year

Application deadline: 06 Jan 2025

We support the treatment of patients in 118 countries with products across three therapeutic areas: Immunotherapy, Haematology, and Critical Care. 

Octapharma has seven R&D sites and five state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Austria, France, Germany and Sweden, and operates more than 190 plasma donation centres across Europe and the US.

descriptionHtml:
Vill du kombinera möjligheterna, som ett globalt företag ger, med fördelarna som ett familjeföretag har? Octapharma är ett bioteknologiskt läkemedelsföretag med storskalig tillverkning på nordvästra Kungsholmen - med vision att förbättra människors hälsa och liv.

Vad kommer du att få göra som Compliance officer?

  • Kontinuerlig uppföljning av GMP-efterlevnaden inom sektionen/avdelningen och tillsammans med arbetsledning
  • Säkerställa att kvalitetsarbetet inom sektionen och Production Unit följer cGMP och gällande myndighetskrav
  • Kontaktperson inom kvalitetsfrågor mot stödfunktioner
  • Utbilda personal och säkerställa implementeringar av instruktioner
  • Leda riskbedömningar vid förändringar med avseende på GMP
  • I samarbete med QiO och sektions/avdelningschef, följa upp avdelningens avvikelser/händelserapporter samt analysera och utvärdera avvikelserna för att proaktivt hitta förbättringar

Vem är du?

  • Naturvetenskaplig högskoleutbildning inom kemi, bioteknik alternativt flerårig relevant arbetslivserfarenhet inom läkemedelsindustrin
  • Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska både skriftligt och muntligt
  • Erfarenhet av att ha jobbat med automatiserade processer och utrustning, meriterande inom en GMP miljö
  • Vi söker en kvalitetsmedveten person som är självständig och kan fatta egna beslut. Du bör vara proaktiv och ta egna initiativ i ditt arbete. Du har en stark förmåga att analysera och lösa problem, och du strävar alltid efter att förbättra arbetsprocesser och resultat. Din förmåga att arbeta effektivt både självständigt och i team är avgörande för att lyckas i denna roll

LVP-55 inom Production Unit

  • Production Unit består av fem avdelningar: Fractionation, Pharmaceutical Production, Biopharmaceutical Production, Validering & Operation Support.
  • Stockholm innerstads enda fabriksmiljö där vi har plasmabaserad samt rekombinant läkemedelstillverkning
  • Produktionen pågår 24 timmar om dygnet, sju dagar i veckan
  • I dagsläget bearbetas cirka 2 800 000 liter blodplasma årligen i Stockholmsfabriken
  • Vi investerar kontinuerligt i vår produktion vilket har medfört en kraftig modernisering och expansion av vår tillverkningskapacitet de senaste åren
  • LVP-55 består av totalt 52 personer och du rapporterar till chefen för sektionen

Vad är det bästa med att jobba hos oss?

  • Du bidrar till att rädda liv - Varje dag är meningsfull då vi tillverkar livräddande läkemedel
  • Förmåner – Bonusprogram, förmånsportal med förmånliga deals och erbjudanden samt naprapat på arbetsplatsen med flera.
  • Familjära värderingar - Långsiktigt perspektiv gällande personal och relationer
  • Bistro bryggeriet – Varje dag serveras hemlagad mat och vi har ett eget bageri/kafé
  • Kompetensutveckling - Vi erbjuder bland annat flertalet olika medarbetar- och ledarskapsutbildningar, både interna och externa, trainee-program samt digitala lösningar

Ansök idag!

Har du frågor om tjänsten, kontakta Annie Eklund (rekryteringsspecialist) på: annie.eklund@octapharma.com. Sista ansökningsdag är 2025-09-30, men tjänsten kan tillsättas dessförinnan.

Om du går vidare i processen

  • Vi vill tidigt i processen samla in så mycket fakta som möjligt för att kunna göra den bästa matchningen
  • Inom några dagar kommer du få utföra arbetspsykologiska tester
  • Testerna görs tidigt i processen eftersom vi vill säkerställa ett objektivt, fördomsfritt och rättvist urvalsarbete

Vill du lära känna oss bättre? Lär mer om Production Unit på vår hemsida och följ oss dag för dag på LinkedIn!

title: Compliance Officer

companyDescription:
Transforming the invoice-to-cash process, Billogram’s intelligent platform 
orchestrates payments, strengthens customer relationships, and reduces churn. It delivers operational efficiency and a seamless payment experience that benefits businesses and their customers.
As we challenge the traditional world of invoicing, working with driven and talented people is a necessity and something Billogram has prioritized since the start. As of today, around 100 ambitious people make up our great team. 

The whole company is driven by a vision of building a service for the future and we have a great time doing so. To continue this journey, we are always looking for people who have new perspectives and like to keep reinventing what is already working.

If you're interested in getting to know us better, check out our career site - career.billogram.com
companySlogan: Easy payments. Quality engagement.
descriptionHtml:
At Billogram we like to be the good guys of billing, making money when clients are happy and pay on time rather than from missed payments. We believe it always pays off to treat people well. That’s why our platform for invoices and payments is built to remove pain, and instead be of gain to both our clients and their customers – improving their relations.


To support our growth and become a leader in the European consumer billing market we are looking for a Payments Support Administrator to join our Payments Support team.


About the role:

As a Payments Support Administrator, you will be a key person in our team, working at the intersection of payments, compliance, and process excellence. The role is a mix of administrative tasks and customer support, where your work has a direct impact on our financial integrity.


You will handle everything from payment allocations and refunds on our client funds account to monitoring transactions to ensure regulatory compliance. Your daily work will be in systems like Zendesk, CM1, and Notion, where you will independently collaborate with clients and their customers as well as other partners to resolve complex problems, and contribute to our internal knowledge base.


Primary responsibilities:

  • Own the payments flow by managing transactions, reconciling accounts, and ensuring compliance with financial regulations.
  • Keep accurate records and documentation related to payments and support cases to ensure transparency and compliance.
  • Analyze and act on transaction data to detect unexpected errors and signals of fraud or suspicious behavior.
  • Proactively drive improvements by identifying opportunities to enhance workflows and efficiency.
  • Collaborate closely with teams across Compliance, Product, and Finance to resolve complex payments and product-related issues.
  • Deliver high-quality support, simplifying complex payment-related topics and ensuring a seamless experience.
  • Stay updated on industry trends, regulations, and best practices to continuously refine how we handle payments and support.


What we are looking for:

You are meticulous and structured, with a strong sense of ownership. You ensure tasks are completed efficiently and accurately. Your ability to quickly notice when something seems off is a great asset, and you naturally take the initiative to investigate or escalate the situation to the right person. You thrive when presented with a complex administrative task and enjoy structuring out the work to get the result or answers necessary.


You are highly analytical and solution-oriented, and you see problems as an opportunity to learn and improve. You are a team player and thrive on collaboration, as you easily find win-win solutions by focusing on common goals.


Your communication is one of your key strengths, and you easily adapt your tone and message to the situation to ensure clarity. While administrative tasks are your core focus, you also appreciate occasional customer contact. You are curious and quick to learn new information, which drives you to continuously improve workflows and processes.


What will help you succeed:

  • At least three years of experience in administrative roles, such as back-office, payment operations, or similar roles involving structured tasks and documentation.
  • Experience working with financial transactions, KYC (Know Your Customer), or AML (Anti-Money Laundering) is a significant plus.
  • Ability to quickly learn and navigate administrative systems, including Zendesk, CM1 and Notion.
  • Fluency in Swedish and English, both written and spoken, is required. Fluency in German is a plus.


Why work with us?

At Billogram we promise you an exciting journey as we break into new markets and grow at a rapid pace. That aside you will get lots of freedom and opportunity to develop together with great colleagues at our office, right in the center of Stockholm. We also offer flexible working hours, exciting challenges and a whole lot of laughs.


We're committed to your long-term development, offering clear career progression paths. You'll also enjoy a fantastic benefits package, including 30 (+2) days of holiday, an occupational pension, a flexible wellness allowance, sick leave top-up, and parental leave compensation. At Billogram, our inclusive and diverse culture ensures that everyone can enjoy a fulfilling career and a great work-life balance.


At Billogram, we provide equal opportunities and strive for an inclusive and diverse environment. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to gender, transgender identity or expression, ethnicity, religion or other beliefs, disability, sexual orientation, or age.


Welcome to the world of Billogram!

companyDescription:
HiQ is a tech consulting company focused on custom software development, positioned at the intersection of digital transformation and specialized engineering. Our 1,700 tech experts and software developers blend innovation, creativity, and deep technical know-how to build outstanding digital solutions.
At HiQ, we don't just consult – we engineer digital transformation to ignite the future and help our clients spearhead their industry. Since 2020, we've been part of the Triton family. Our home base is in Sweden, Finland, and Germany, but our digital footprints? They're moonwalking across the globe!
companySlogan: We fuse tech and creativity to engineer cutting-edge solutions and future-proof businesses across the globe.
descriptionHtml:
Nu söker vi fler kollegor till vårt växande Cybersecurity-team på HiQ. Här finns möjligheten att tillsammans med ett starkt team hjälpa intressanta företag, och i förlängningen privatpersoner, att leva i en säkrare digital framtid. På HiQ arbetar vi i framkant med cybersäkerhet, under en nationell satsning inom organisationen bygger vi upp ett Centre of Excellence i Stockholm. Vårt mål är att etablera en stark cybersäkerhetsleverans som skapar trygghet inom organisationer och företag.

På HiQ finns en möjlighet att vara med och bygga något stort. Det är en lärorik och rolig resa vi har framför oss där vår spelplan är enorm och du kan göra ordentliga avtryck i massor av olika bolag och branscher. Vi kommer värdera din erfarenhet och dina tankar om hur vi på bästa sätt kan bygga vidare området. Vi har en bred kundbas där du får möjlighet att testa olika branscher och får uppdrag hos både kunder med ett väletablerat cybersäkerhetsarbete samt hos kunder i kritiska faser där vår erfarenhet är helt avgörande.

Vi söker dig som har:

  • Flerårig erfarenhet inom cybersäkerhet, t.ex. inom något av följande:
    • Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC)
    • Identitets- och behörighetsstyrning (IAM)
    • Informationssäkerhetshantering
    • Standarder som DORA, CRA, NIS2 m.fl.
Om HiQ

På HiQ välkomnar vi alla typer av kollegor – det viktigaste är att du gillar utmaningar och är en schysst lagspelare. Vi består av utvecklare, arkitekter, agila projektledare, UX/UI-designers, cyberspecialister m.fl. – en härlig mix av kompetenser, perspektiv och personligheter som stärker oss varje dag. Vi växer ständigt med ny energi, och här får du både utveckla och utvecklas. Du får förtroendet att prova först och lära sedan, och vi bygger HiQ tillsammans – ett bolag vi själva vill jobba på.

Vi tror på att vara riktigt bra på det vi gör, men också på att ha jäkligt kul längs vägen. Vår kultur är drivande – inte styrande – vilket gör att du kan vara dig själv till 100 %. Det gör att vi tar vårt jobb seriöst, men inte alltid oss själva. Och vi menar det när vi säger att bra stämning leder till bra resultat.

I denna rekrytering tillämpar vi löpande urval och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan ansökningstiden har gått ut. Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!

companyDescription:
At American Express, we know that with the right backing, people and businesses have the power to progress in incredible ways. Whether we’re supporting our customers’ financial confidence to move ahead, taking commerce to new heights, or encouraging people to explore the world, our colleagues are constantly striving to uphold our powerful backing promise to our customers and each other every day. 
 
These beliefs have been our North Star for 170 years as our business transformed – from helping evacuate travelers during World Wars, to ensuring the safety of our customers’ funds during the Great Depression in the U.S., to creating the Shop Small® movement to help small businesses recover from the Financial Crisis, to providing aid to communities impacted by many natural disasters and so much more.  
 
For generations, the key to our success has been the determination and resilience of our American Express colleagues. Now, as a globally integrated payments company, we work together to provide customers with access to products, insights and world-class experiences that enrich lives and build business success. Join us and let’s lead the way together. 

Learn more about us at: 
https://www.americanexpress.com/careers
https://www.americanexpress.com/
https://www.facebook.com/AmericanExpressUS
https://www.instagram.com/americanexpress/
https://twitter.com/americanexpress
https://www.youtube.com/user/AmericanExpress

See our community guidelines at: 
https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/company/community-guidelines/

If you have a customer service issue or question, please visit www.americanexpress.com/contactus
descriptionHtml:
At American Express, our culture is built on a 175-year history of innovation, shared values and Leadership Behaviors, and an unwavering commitment to back our customers, communities, and colleagues. As part of Team Amex, you'll experience this powerful backing with comprehensive support for your holistic well-being and many opportunities to learn new skills, develop as a leader, and grow your career.
 

Here, your voice and ideas matter, your work makes an impact, and together, you will help us define the future of American Express.
 

American Express is a Global company with a strong presence and growing card customer base in the Nordics. We, the Nordic and Baltic Global Merchant and Network Services, (GM&NS), based in Stockholm, are responsible for maintaining and growing the merchant base to support those customers. 

How will you make an impact in this role?
The role of Legal Entity Manager is a critical role for the business, reporting directly to the General Manager (GM). The role includes providing regulatory and operational support, and implementations, to the Nordics GM&NS business, and explicitly keeping the GM updated and well informed. It also includes representing business interest in risk committees for the Nordics markets, as well as partnering closely with business, compliance and operational teams. All to ensure a compliant and business focused operation.




Principle Responsibility:

  • Leading the implementation of new, or changed, regulatory requirements across several markets and channels
  • Operative support for updates of terms, forms, policies, and other documents
  • First point of contact and ownership for internal audits and support to GM for any external audits
  • Point of liaison between the market and central operative teams to drive central initiatives locally
  • Lead operational risk efforts—own remediation plans, track progress, and drive projects
  • Represent business interests in legal entity governance and collaborate closely with governance structures
  • Coordinating second-line testing and managing data requirements
  • Project Management of regulatory local projects and ad-hoc compliance projects supporting the GM

  • Collaborate with e.g. compliance, legal, and other central teams to ensure alignment, compliance, effective processes, and business-minded solutions
     
     
     
     

    Minimum Qualifications
     

    •    Excellent communication skills and fluent proficiency both verbal and written in English and Swedish, some knowledge of Finnish is an advantage 
    •    Bachelor's degree in relevant fields of study or equivalent experience
    •    Ability to manage complex projects and run multiple initiatives in parallel with a strong sense of planning
    •    Attention to detail and rigor given the complexities involved in the content
    •    Ability to build and leverage relationships 
    •    Ability to challenge the status quo and take decisions independently when required
    •    Strong excel skills to manage and manipulate data from multiple sources 
     
     
     

    Preferred Qualifications
    •    Previous experience working with KYC / AML
    •    Prior legal knowledge from firm or company
    •    Proactive problem solver with a 'can-do' attitude
    •    Positive approach with ability to work under pressure
     
     




Non-considerations for sponsorship: 
Employment eligibility to work with American Express in Sweden is required as the company will not pursue visa sponsorship for these positions. 

 

We back you with benefits that support your holistic well-being so you can be and deliver your best. This means caring for you and your loved ones' physical, financial, and mental health, as well as providing the flexibility you need to thrive personally and professionally:
 
  • Competitive base salaries 
  • Bonus incentives 
  • Support for financial-well-being and retirement 
  • Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and disability benefits (depending on location) 
  • Flexible working model with hybrid, onsite or virtual arrangements depending on role and business need 
  • Generous paid parental leave policies (depending on your location) 
  • Free access to global on-site wellness centers staffed with nurses and doctors (depending on location) 
  • Free and confidential counseling support through our Healthy Minds program 
  • Career development and training opportunities
 

Offer of employment with American Express is conditioned upon the successful completion of a background verification check, subject to applicable laws and regulations.

This means caring for you and your loved ones' physical, financial, and mental health, as well as providing the flexibility you need to thrive personally and professionally: Competitive base salaries Bonus incentives Support for financial-well-being and retirement Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and disability benefits (depending on location) Flexible working model with hybrid, onsite or virtual arrangements depending on role and business need Generous paid parental leave policies (depending on your location) Free access to global on-site wellness centers staffed with nurses and doctors (depending on location) Free and confidential counseling support through our Healthy Minds program Career development and training opportunities Offer of employment with American Express is conditioned upon the successful completion of a background verification check, subject to applicable laws and regulations.

companyDescription: Modern banking. Real-time control. Juni provides business banking, spend management, accounting and financing, on one powerful platform and app.
companySlogan: Modern business banking. Real-time control.
descriptionHtml:
Juni who? 👋

At Juni, we’re busy building the future of banking. We want to help businesses do more with less and get access to the financial tools they need to operate, grow, and win globally.

How & Why We Do It 🚀

We give our people the same thing we’re building for our customers: freedom. Freedom to be 100% yourself. Freedom to explore your potential and what’s more – make the most of it. We truly believe we’re making the world a better place for online businesses, and we want you to be a part of that mission.

Your role at a glance 🤸‍♀️

Join our dynamic team at Juni as a Compliance Officer or Senior Compliance officer. You will play a crucial role in ensuring our operations adhere to all relevant regulatory requirements, supporting our mission to provide secure and compliant financial services. This role offers the opportunity to work in a fast-paced environment, contributing to both strategic and operational compliance initiatives.

Your Responsibilities 👏

In this role, you’ll:

  • Implement and oversee monitoring programs to ensure adherence to regulatory requirements and industry standards
  • Conduct compliance risk assessments and monitoring controls
  • Provide guidance and support on compliance-related queries
  • Participate and conduct New Product Approval Process (NPAP) assessments
  • Design and deliver training programs to enhance compliance awareness among employees
  • Manage Juni’s data privacy related controls from a second line perspective
  • Monitor and participate in incident handling
  • Draft and review Juni’s policies, ensuring alignment with current regulations
  • Prepare and submit compliance and regulatory reports to authorities, senior management and the Board as required
  • Secretary and point of contact to Juni’s Governance, Risk & Compliance Committee
  • Act as the internal liaison between internal audit and the rest of the business

Tooling ⚙️

  • Slack
  • G-suite
  • Linear
  • Miro
  • Steep
  • Sana LMS

Qualifications 👑

What we need to see

  • Fluent in Swedish and English
  • Must reside in Sweden
  • Law degree
  • Min. 2 years of experience in a second line of defense role within the financial sector
  • Min. 2 years of experience in an AML/CTF first line of defense role within the financial sector
  • Strong understanding of regulatory requirements and industry standard
  • Practical experience with payment related regulations

What we’d love to see

  • Previous experience as a Data Protection Officer (DPO)
  • Prior experience in fraud prevention, either in the first or second line of defense
  • Previous experience in sanctions domain

Your people 👥

Our team is as ambitious as our amazing customers. We aim high and we move with speed to make our vision a reality. We care deeply about building a better future for our customers and each other. Here, you can work with people at the top of their game and who didn’t get there by playing games. You can help us create a whole new category in financial services.

Your Benefits 😍

We’re freedom-first. Transparent. Caring. Empowering. So our benefits are too.

  • We work hybrid. We'll see you in one of our offices in Stockholm or Gothenburg at least two days per week.
  • Swap 2D for 3D. Meet all Junis IRL at the company onsite each year.
  • Diversity is at our core. We're part Swedish. Part Canadian. Part French. Part Indian. Part Italian. Part British. Part Portuguese. You get the idea.
  • Great players can stay great players. Progress your career whether you choose to manage people or not.
  • Stock options. We can’t promise you’ll make a fortune. But we’ll give it our very best shot.
  • Vacation days. 30 days. Awesome.
  • Private Health insurance. You know. Just in case.
  • Beautiful offices in central Gothenburg and Stockholm, front row sea view!

At Juni, we’re building the future of banking. We want to help businesses do more with less and get access to the financial tools they need to operate, grow, and win globally.

companyDescription:
Ricoh Sverige AB erbjuder heltäckande lösningar för en flexibel och agil, digital arbetsplats genom ett brett utbud av hårdvaror, mjukvaror, tjänster och konsultverksamhet. Bolaget har specialiserad expertis inom bland annat IT-tjänster, dokument- och utskriftshantering, virtuella mötes- och kommunikationslösningar samt grafisk kommunikation. Ricoh finns representerat i ca 200 länder men även nationellt på över 30 orter med egna Center och Partner, samt ett återförsäljarnät. 

Företaget är kvalitets-, miljö- och informationssäkerhetscertifierat enligt ISO 9001, 14001 samt 27001. Ricoh Sverige är ett dotterbolag i den världsomspännande Ricoh-koncernen.

About Ricoh 

Ricoh is empowering digital workplaces using innovative technologies and services enabling individuals to work smarter. For more than 80 years, Ricoh has been driving innovation and is a leading provider of document management solutions, IT services, commercial and industrial printing, digital cameras, and industrial systems. 

Headquartered in Tokyo, Ricoh Group operates in approximately 200 countries and regions. In the financial year ended March 2017, Ricoh Group had worldwide sales of 2,028 billion yen (approx. 18.2 billion USD). For further information, please visit www.ricoh.se.
companySlogan: A Digital Services Company. Leading Change at Work.
descriptionHtml:
Change your job, change your workplace, change your future...


We are actively building diverse teams and welcome applications from everyone

Role: Compliance Executive at Ricoh Sweden

Located: Solna Sweden (Ricoh operate a vibrant working policy giving you flexible hybrid working options)

Package: Competitive salary, pension, and benefits



What you will be doing


We are looking for a Compliance & CSR Executive for Ricoh Sweden who wants to drive sustainability work forward within the company. We hope you want to make a difference and be part of an exciting journey - to secure Ricoh's position as a leading provider of sustainable document and information management as well as communication technology.


Key Responsibilities:

As a Compliance & CSR Executive , you will work with a wide range of tasks and be the company's contact person within Compliance & CSR. Your tasks will include:

  • Initiating, driving, and participating in projects and ensuring that relevant processes for Compliance are implemented in the organization.
  • Planning, prioritizing, and coordinating the company's management system activities, as well as being responsible for certification, maintenance, administration, and development of the system.
  • Ensuring compliance with laws and other requirements within the areas of responsibility for Compliance.
  • Leading and setting requirements for the work to implement the requirements of ISO 9001, 14001, and 27001 in the company's processes and routines.
  • Planning and conducting internal and external audits of the company's management system.
  • Ultimately responsible for ensuring that current information within the areas of responsibility for Compliance is available to the organization.
  • Ensuring that the organization can provide customers with product-related information on sustainability.
  • Supporting and, when necessary, answering customer and tender questions within the department's areas of responsibility.



You will ideally have


You have a relevant post-secondary education or equivalent work experience in Compliance. We wish that you have documented training in auditing according to ISO 9001, 14001, and/or 27001 and that you have led internal audits. Experience in project management (or training), good knowledge of environmental impacts and environmental legislation, and experience from producing companies and business-to-business are also desired.

As a person, it is important that you have a lot of drive, do not give up, and are good at anchoring ideas. The ideal candidate must be confident in your competence and hold strong integrity. You should be good at working independently, but it is also important that you can collaborate with others both internally and externally. Being able to work both in the short and long term is important, as well as being able to prioritise. You should be able to write and express yourself both in Swedish and English and be able to create presentations and have good presentation skills.


You are offered


An exciting role in a global corporation. For those who want to develop, there are all opportunities, both nationally and internationally. Competence development is a natural part of everyday life as you constantly exchange ideas and experiences with your colleagues. Ricoh is located in very nice premises on Solna Strandväg in Solna.

We review applications and conduct interviews continuously - so do not wait to send in your application!


About the company


Ricoh Sverige AB is a leading company in document and information management as well as communication technology. Ricoh Sweden reports to Ricoh Europe in London, which is the headquarters for Europe, the EMEA region. The global headquarters is in Tokyo, Japan, and is listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange. The Ricoh Group offers solutions for customers' IT environments with a wide range of equipment, software, services, and consulting. Ricoh Sweden is a Swedish subsidiary with about 200 employees and is part of the global Ricoh Group. The total turnover in Sweden amounts to nearly 900 million SEK (FY 2019/2020). Globally, the Ricoh Group has approximately 90,000 employees in 200 countries, with sustainability as an important focus. All work we do at Ricoh is based on our corporate philosophy, Ricoh Way. This includes the seven basic principles; Team Work, Customer Centric, Innovation, Winning Spirit, Passion, Gemba, and Ethics and Integrity. We believe it is important to take care of each other and to take care of our planet! The result of our work is an innovative company that works hard to reduce environmental impact and to be an active part of society through various CSR projects. Please visit the website for more information about Ricoh, www.ricoh.se

companyDescription:
Rättvist byggande är en ideell förening öppen för alla långsiktiga byggherrar som på riktigt vill bidra till sund konkurrens och brottsfria byggen. 

Rättvist byggande är också vår arbetsmodell där byggherrar samverkar operativt och på så sätt skyddar varandra från risker kopplat till kriminalitet och arbetsrättsliga brister i sina byggprojekt.

Samverkan sker genom att byggherrarna bildat en ideell förening som utvecklat metoder och system som används i byggprojekten. Det ger medlemmarna större förmåga att upptäcka, motverka och förebygga riskerna än vad man kan skapa själva. Läs mer på vår hemsida.
companySlogan: Byggherrar i samverkan för sund konkurrens och brottsfria byggen
descriptionHtml:
Rättvist byggande söker drivande Compliance officer

Vi arbetar för en byggbransch fri från arbetslivskriminalitet. Vi är en idéburen verksamhet utan vinstintresse som bildats som ett strategiskt och operativt verktyg mellan stora, målmedvetna beställare av byggprojekt. Medlemmarna hos har både skyldigheter och rättigheter. Tillsammans utgör vi en av de största inköpsvolymerna av byggarbeten i Sverige. Vi har satt standarden för hur byggbranschen arbetar mot fusk av olika slag på byggarbetsplatser och vi har fått en tongivande roll inom socialt hållbart arbete i branschen. Vårt arbete går ut på att vi är experter på att upptäcka, motverka och förebygga fusk och social dumping genom industriella och affärsmässiga metoder. Vår hävstång är våra medlemmars beställarmakt i kombination med våra medarbetares kompetens och förmåga.

Vi vill ha modiga och ödmjuka medarbetare som är passionerade för vår vision. Våra medarbetare ska ha en egen inre vilja att uppnå höga resultat och disciplinen att lyckas med det. Alla våra medarbetar ska ha potential att bli bäst i Sverige på sina respektive ansvarsområden.”

Vi söker nu en utåtriktad, orädd Compliance officer med engagemang för samhället och för rättvisa villkor

Vad är det ens för ett ord? Compliance officer? Nja, titlar är egentligen oviktiga för oss.

Vi söker en person som vill använda sin hjärna och driv med hjärtat som kompass. Arbetet som Compliance officer handlar om att vara en motor hos medlemsföretagen och få vår modell att fungera i praktiken. Allt ifrån att utbilda, stötta, ställa krav i upphandlingar till att planera genomförandet av arbetsplatskontroller på byggen samt driva avtalsåtgärder. En del av arbetet är också att stötta förändringsresan hos vår medlem så att de stärker sin egen organisations förmåga och bli mindre beroende av oss. En annan del är att bevaka att medlemmarna följer föreningens stadgar.

I arbetet ingår många kontakter med projektledare och chefer hos medlemmar och entreprenörer. Du behöver både vara ute på byggen, hos medlemsföretagen och på vårt kontor. Du förväntas bidra till vår egen utveckling som organisation med dina kunskaper och lärdomar.

Du ingår i ett team av experter med passion för vår vision men driver mycket arbete självständigt. I arbetet krävs det integritet, drivkraft och relationsskapande.

Egenskaper för att trivas och lyckas hos oss är:

  • Värderingsstyrd: Genuin, inre vilja att skapa rättvisa villkor för företag och människor
  • Eget driv: Tydligt eget driv att arbeta med förändring/förbättring
  • Integritet och mod för att kunna hantera sekretess och stå fast vid beslut trots motstånd
  • Vi vill gärna ha en samlande kraft och en relationsbyggande kollega i gruppen

Kompetenser som vi tror är värdefulla att ha med sig:

  • Vana att arbeta med byggentreprenader och byggavtal
  • Förmåga att kunna kommunicera på formellt och korrekt skriftspråk på svenska
  • Erfarenhet från arbete i eller nära offentlig verksamhet

Vi märker att vi är bäst när vi träffas regelbundet, därför är arbetet förlagt på plats men möjlighet finns att arbeta hemifrån någon då och då.

Placering: vårt kontor i Stockholm (resor kan förekomma)

Omfattning: Heltid

Anställningsform: Tillsvidare

Om du är intresserad efter att ha läst ovanstående så tar vi gärna emot din ansökan (cv och ett kort brev per e-post). I ansökan behöver du svara på dessa frågor.

  • Varför vill du arbeta för Rättvist byggande?
  • Vad är det hos dig som gör att du skulle passa i denna yrkesroll?
  • Vad innebär ”rättvisa” för dig (kopplat till vårt arbete)?
  • Godkänner du att vi genomför en bakgrundskontroll på dig?

Det kan tillkomma ett enklare arbetsprov under rekryteringsprocessen.

Ansökningsdatum:

Just nu har vi en öppen ansökan och tar intervjuer löpande. Vi kommer inte att intervjua under sommaren men läser ansökningarna som kommer in.

companyDescription: Froda is a tech company committed to empowering entrepreneurs by simplifying access to financing for all small businesses. We use digitization and machine learning to break down traditional financial barriers, ensuring unbiased support for founders. Our data-driven approach promotes innovation, diversity, and inclusivity, significantly impacting the communities we serve.
companySlogan: Dedicated to empowering entrepreneurs by simplifying access to financing for all SMEs globally.
descriptionHtml:
We’re looking for a driven and skilled Data Scientist to design, deploy, and optimize ML models that drive automation and impact key areas like credit risk and fraud detection. Join us to shape the future of ML at Froda!

About Froda

Froda was founded in 2015 with a clear mission – to democratize the access to financing and provide small businesses the same opportunities to grow as large ones. Through digital processes and technological innovation, we have shortened the loan process from months to minutes, making it easy and affordable for business owners to invest in their ideas. Our objective approach champions diversity, ensures fair terms, and ultimately enables more small businesses to make a positive impact on society.

Join our team and contribute to giving small businesses the opportunity to invest in their ideas to the best possible terms.

Your Impact

Design, build, and deploy machine learning models to drive automation and efficiency across areas like credit risk, fraud detection, AML, customer service, and business analytics. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to build scalable, robust solutions that support Froda’s strategic goals of scalability, standardization, and reliability.

What You'll Do

  • Collaborate with product, engineering, and analytics teams to deliver scalable and impactful AI and ML solutions.
  • Design, build, and deploy ML models and pipelines for critical business areas.
  • Implement and optimize ML-Ops workflows (data processing, model training, deployment, and monitoring).

What You'll Bring

  • Experience of working as a Data Scientist or ML Engineer in tech, fintech, or a similar product-driven company.
  • Hands-on experience with production ML pipelines, ML-Ops frameworks, and cloud platforms (e.g., KubeFlow, Azure).
  • Proven skills in Python, SQL, and ML libraries (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, etc.).
  • Experience with LLMs (e.g., GPT APIs, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, RAG).
  • Knowledge of credit risk, fraud detection, or AML is a clear plus.

Who You Are

  • Results-driven problem solver with a passion for impact through data and AI and that thrives in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.
  • Collaborative team player, contributing to innovation and knowledge-sharing in the team and across the business.
  • Strong communicator, able to break down complex ideas into clear insights.

What’s in it for you?

Working at Froda means working at a company that combines the best of both worlds – fun and fast-paced culture, yet stable and structured. We are 90+ ambitious self-starters who genuinely care for each other and the work we do, while having fun together. You will have the ability to impact your own work and that the only thing standing between you and your goals is going to be your own ambitions.

Furthermore, you’ll get:

  • Fabulous office overlooking Stockholm and a friendly culture that values work/life balance
  • We prioritize working in the office to maximize the positive impact of in-person collaboration, but we offer flexibility with work hours and remote work
  • Competitive compensation package
  • Insurance and pension plan

Be part of a growing team where your work directly impacts the company’s success. Join us in driving meaningful change through AI and machine learning.

Please note that due to summer vacations, our response time may be delayed.

companyDescription: Modern banking. Real-time control. Juni provides business banking, spend management, accounting and financing, on one powerful platform and app.
companySlogan: Modern business banking. Real-time control.
descriptionHtml:
Juni who? 👋
At Juni, we’re busy building the future of banking. We want to help businesses do more with less and get access to the financial tools they need to operate, grow, and win globally.
How & Why We Do It 🚀
We give our people the same thing we’re building for our customers: freedom. Freedom to be 100% yourself. Freedom to explore your potential and what’s more – make the most of it. We truly believe we’re making the world a better place for online businesses, and we want you to be a part of that mission.
Your role at a glance 🤸‍♀️
Join our dynamic team at Juni as a Compliance Officer or Senior Compliance officer. You will play a crucial role in ensuring our operations adhere to all relevant regulatory requirements, supporting our mission to provide secure and compliant financial services. This role offers the opportunity to work in a fast-paced environment, contributing to both strategic and operational compliance initiatives.
Your Responsibilities 👏
In this role, you’ll:
  • Implement and oversee monitoring programs to ensure adherence to regulatory requirements and industry standards
  • Conduct compliance risk assessments and monitoring controls
  • Provide guidance and support on compliance-related queries
  • Participate and conduct New Product Approval Process (NPAP) assessments
  • Design and deliver training programs to enhance compliance awareness among employees
  • Manage Juni’s data privacy related controls from a second line perspective
  • Monitor and participate in incident handling
  • Draft and review Juni’s policies, ensuring alignment with current regulations
  • Prepare and submit compliance and regulatory reports to authorities, senior management and the Board as required
  • Secretary and point of contact to Juni’s Governance, Risk & Compliance Committee
  • Act as the internal liaison between internal audit and the rest of the business
Tooling ⚙️
  • Slack
  • G-suite
  • Linear
  • Miro
  • Steep
  • Sana LMS
Qualifications 👑
What we need to see
  • Fluent in Swedish and English
  • Must reside in Sweden
  • Law degree
  • Min. 2 years of experience in a second line of defense role within the financial sector
  • Min. 2 years of experience in an AML/CTF first line of defense role within the financial sector
  • Strong understanding of regulatory requirements and industry standard
  • Practical experience with payment related regulations
What we’d love to see
  • Previous experience as a Data Protection Officer (DPO)
  • Prior experience in fraud prevention, either in the first or second line of defense
  • Previous experience in sanctions domain
Your people 👥
Our team is as ambitious as our amazing customers. We aim high and we move with speed to make our vision a reality. We care deeply about building a better future for our customers and each other. Here, you can work with people at the top of their game and who didn’t get there by playing games. You can help us create a whole new category in financial services.
Your Benefits 😍
We’re freedom-first. Transparent. Caring. Empowering. So our benefits are too.
  • We work hybrid. We'll see you in one of our offices in Stockholm or Gothenburg at least two days per week.
  • Swap 2D for 3D. Meet all Junis IRL at the company onsite each year.
  • Diversity is at our core. We're part Swedish. Part Canadian. Part French. Part Indian. Part Italian. Part British. Part Portuguese. You get the idea.
  • Great players can stay great players. Progress your career whether you choose to manage people or not.
  • Stock options. We can’t promise you’ll make a fortune. But we’ll give it our very best shot.
  • Vacation days. 30 days. Awesome.
  • Private Health insurance. You know. Just in case.
  • Beautiful offices in central Gothenburg and Stockholm, front row sea view!
At Juni, we’re building the future of banking. We want to help businesses do more with less and get access to the financial tools they need to operate, grow, and win globally.

companyDescription:
LeoVegas Group is a leading international igaming company with a clear vision to create the world’s greatest igaming experience. The Group offers online casino, live casino, and sports betting via 9 brands in 10 jurisdictions. The Group continues to grow rapidly, currently employing over 1,400 people in Europe, including at its headquarters in Stockholm and operations hub in Malta.

As one of the most innovative companies in the industry, the Group also invests and develops other igaming companies through its investment arm, LeoVentures. In 2022, LeoVegas Group became a subsidiary of the global entertainment company MGM Resorts International (NYSE: MGM). 

For more information, visit www.leovegasgroup.com.
descriptionHtml:
Description

Position at LeoVegas Group

About The Role

Are you a strategic compliance leader with a sharp mind, a sense of humour, and a passion for enabling responsible business growth? Do you thrive on solving complex problems, building strong teams, and bridging the gap between compliance obligations and risk and commercial opportunity? If so - LeoVegas Group wants you.

LeoVegas is a leading GameTech group at the cutting edge of mobile gaming innovation, operating globally through powerful brands like LeoVegas and BetMGM. Since being acquired by MGM Resorts International in 2022, we’ve been on a steep trajectory of transformation and expansion -and now we’re building a powerhouse Nordic compliance function to match.

This is a rare opportunity to take the reins of compliance across the Nordics (Sweden, Denmark, and soon, Finland) - leading the way not just in governance, but in shaping the way compliance supports commercial success.

As Head of Compliance – Nordics, you’ll be the go-to expert for senior Nordic management on all things compliance for our Nordic markets. You’ll lead a regional team (which factors in both dotted and direct line reports from the wider LeCo support function), sit at the table with commercial decision-makers, and be a critical link between the wider business and LeCo.

This isn’t about saying "no" - it’s about knowing how to say “yes,” responsibly.

YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR:

  • Build, lead and enable smooth processes and communication for a high-performing Nordic dedicated compliance team, covering Sweden, Denmark, and (upon licensing) Finland.
  • Be the bridge between business and compliance: guiding commercial teams on regulatory obligations and embedding compliance into business decisions and working closely with other regional compliance heads to ensure consistency of Group reporting and risk.
  • Partner closely with other verticals in LeCo - Privacy, Legal, Technical Compliance, AML Governance - to ensure cohesion and clarity and “one voice” from LeCo towards the business functions.
  • Represent the Group with Nordic regulators and lead proactive engagement alongside our Policy & Regulatory Affairs team.
  • Monitor and report on regional compliance and identify and take accountability for when issues or risks require central clearance, escalation, or consultation with the Group’s central LeCo functions rather than being decided in the regional management team ensuring transparency and alignment across Group.
  • Stay ahead of regulatory developments, shape internal processes, and ensure we're always a step ahead.
  • Own and manage all Nordic reporting obligations, including those to local regulators and MGM Resorts International.
  • Maintain a “business partner” mindset - solving problems, not creating roadblocks.
  • Promote collaboration across the Group to avoid silos and build consistent, scalable practices.

OUR SUCCESSFUL CANDIDATE WILL HAVE THE FOLLOWING:

  • A Master’s degree in Law or a related field.
  • 5+ years’ experience in a senior compliance or regulatory role.
  • Proven experience influencing policy, engaging regulators, and contributing to consultation responses.
  • A track record of working closely with commercial teams and translating complex regulatory matters into practical business solutions.
  • A passion for building and enabling teams.
  • A sense of humour, resilience under pressure, and a team-first mindset.
  • Native-level proficiency in Swedish.

Who We Are

At the core of LeoVegas Group is Team Leo. Our culture is our foundation and is what enables us to innovate, build, and lead as we trailblaze our way through the igaming industry. We’re a team of over 2000 innovators, initiators, and groundbreakers working in a fast-paced and agile environment across 19 offices worldwide.

Benefits

  • Hybrid work policy
  • 4 weeks of Workation (T&C apply)
  • 30 annual vacation days
  • Occupational Pension
  • 5,000 SEK wellness contribution annually
  • Parental Leave Top-Up
  • Possibility to enrol in a private health care insurance for both you and your partner
  • 1,500 SEK equipment allowance
  • Benify - benefits portal with many offers and discounts
  • We offer a relocation package with accommodation and flights.

JOIN US!

In our pride, we empower our teammates to find their roar and run with their wildest ideas. We don’t wait for things to happen; we pounce and make it happen!

Would you be a good fit for the Leo Pride - give us a roar!

**As our company working language is English, we’d like to see your CV in English, please**

_________________________________________________________________________________________________

companyDescription:
ROYC is a leading B2B financial technology company that provides a complete private markets operating system, empowering private equity firms, banks, wealth managers, and multi-family offices to seamlessly access, distribute, and manage private investments at scale.
  
As private markets expand, financial institutions require scalable, technology-driven solutions to manage complexity, optimizing fund operations, and delivering exceptional client experiences.

ROYC combines state-of-the art private markets technology with tailored fund structuring and investment services. Our intuitive, scalable platform replaces manual processes with automation and real-time data access, transforming how private market investments are managed across the entire fund lifecycle.
companySlogan: Investment Operating System. Private Markets Investments at Scale.
descriptionHtml:
About ROYC

ROYC is the leading European B2B financial technology company that provides a complete private markets operating system, empowering private equity firms, banks, wealth managers, and multi-family offices to seamlessly access, distribute, and manage private investments at scale. As private markets expand, financial institutions require scalable, technology-driven solutions to manage complexity, optimizing fund operations, and delivering exceptional client experiences. ROYC combines state-of-the-art private markets technology with tailored fund structuring and investment solutions. Its intuitive, scalable platform replaces manual processes with automation and real-time data access, transforming how private market investments are managed across the entire fund lifecycle.

Role Purpose

As an Investor Service Officer at ROYC, you will serve as the primary point of contact for our sophisticated investor base. You’ll ensure a seamless experience from onboarding through ongoing support—managing accounts, coordinating transactions, liaising with external administrators, and partnering closely with both Sales and Technology teams to continuously enhance our platform.

Role Description

  • Investor Onboarding & Support
    • Guide new investors through account setup, KYC/AML checks, and documentation
    • Respond promptly to inquiries on transactions, statements, and platform functionality
  • Third-Party Relationship Management
    • Own day-to-day interactions with fund administrators, depositories, and distribution partners
    • Monitor service levels, escalate issues, and drive continuous improvement
  • Cross-Functional Partnership
    • Collaborate with Sales to support investor pitches and proposal follow-up
    • Work alongside Tech to surface user feedback, prioritize enhancements, and test new features
  • Process Optimization & Compliance
    • Embed best practices in processing procedures, controls, and reporting
    • Identify gaps, propose solutions, and implement process improvements across the investment organization
What We’re Looking For

  • Experience
  • 2–5 years in private equity, venture capital, or fund administration—servicing HNWIs or institutional clients
  • Domain Knowledge
  • Solid grasp of investor due diligence requirements, fund structures, and private-market products
  • Technical Savvy
  • Proficiency with CRM tools, investor platforms, and Excel modelling
  • Communication & Service Excellence
  • Exceptional verbal and written skills in English (French fluency a plus)
  • Demonstrated track record of delivering high-touch, client-centric experiences
  • Personal Attributes
  • Rigorous attention to detail and strong organizational skills
  • Problem-solver with tenacity to thrive in a fast-paced environment
  • Collaborative mindset and intellectual curiosity to help build a market-leading platform

Key Competencies

  • Problem Solving: Analyze and resolve investor issues quickly and effectively
  • Teamwork: Build strong partnerships across Sales, Operations, Compliance, and Technology
  • Time Management: Prioritize tasks and meet deadlines without sacrificing quality
  • Client Feedback: Actively solicit, interpret, and act on investor insights to enhance service levels
  • Accountability: Take initiative and ownership over your work and relationships

Preferred Profile

  • Recent experience of private equity or venture capital either for an investment firm or fund administrator.
  • Strong understanding of investor due diligence requirements, investment products, and fund structures within the private markets.
  • Good organizational and administration skills with high attention to detail.
  • Strong interpersonal skills.
  • Strong oral and written communication skills. Preferably English and French
  • Excellent client service skills
  • Driven and intellectually curious, having an ambition to play a key role in building a market leading solution in the private markets.

companyDescription:
The Web Manuals document management system supports aviation professionals with the Editing, Distribution and Compliance Monitoring of their manuals with agility and control. 

We work with a global perspective building partnerships with the world's leading experts and suppliers in aviation compliance management. 

Web Manuals Store includes complementary products and services such as templates manuals and compliance linking. 

We support over 620 operations in all segments of aviation. Book a demo to find out how we can help you take control of your documentation. We'd love to chat with you!
companySlogan: The All-in-One Document Management System For Aviation Professionals
descriptionHtml:
Hey there! We are Web Manuals, a fast-growing SaaS company working towards digitizing the aviation industry. Both our customers and our product is constantly in the clouds but for us the sky is not the limit! We want to continue to grow until we reach the stars but as a bootstrapped company, we do it on our own terms. We are a young, fun, and diverse team that loves to explore new places together. Apply today to be part of our global team!

Web Manuals is truly a place where you can grow, both professionally and personally, and where you can explore the world and try your wings in an innovative environment. We offer an international setting with offices in Malmö (HQ), San Diego, New York, Sydney and Singapore. We keep on growing - and are now looking for a Product Compliance Associate to join our team in Malmö!

About The Role

As a Product Compliance Associate, you will play a vital role in our journey towards digitizing aviation. Your main task will be to quality assure and transfer regulations, standards, and documentation into Web Manuals.

Core responsibilities includes, but are not limited to:

  • To monitor direct sources of the Compliance Libraries and ensure that new content is fed into our database.
  • Update and maintain Compliance Libraries with data from Store partners.
  • To keep internally controlled Libraries up to date.
  • Ensuring the accuracy and quality of our Compliance Libraries content.
  • To assist in feeding improvements into the backlog regarding compliance features and product updates.
  • To assist in improving the Compliance Import Process as needed.

To thrive in this role, we believe that you have:

  • A university degree or equivalent education.
  • A structured approach to work.
  • Excellent collaboration and teamwork skills.
  • Keen attention to detail.
  • Self-motivation and a growth mindset.
  • Strong organizational skills.
  • Clear and professional communication skills in English, both in speech and in writing as English is our corporate language

Ideally, you also have:

  • Proficiency in Word, Excel, and similar tools.
  • Experience from a similar role.

As a person, we believe that you are:

  • Quality-minded and well-organized.
  • Eager to learn and develop.
  • Enthusiastic about working in a dynamic team environment.

Additional information:

Start date: ASAP

Work extent: Full-time

Available positions: 1

Location: Malmö

Other information: We utilize Predictive Index (PI) assessment tool in all our recruitment processes. By leveraging PI, we aim to ensure a strong alignment between the candidate's competencies and our company culture, while minimizing potential biases in decision-making

companyDescription:
emagine is a high-end business & IT consulting company challenging the way businesses leverage high-end expertise.

Enabled by our vast network of expert consultants, we tailor our services to the unique needs of each business, powering progress, solving challenges, and delivering real results.

As the world evolves, scalability becomes increasingly important in modern, technology-driven organizations. And it is around this critical need that emagine stands out with a unique business model and delivery capacity.

With emagine, companies gain the flexibility needed to navigate and succeed in a complex and ever-changing technological landscape. Through a seamless and tailored delivery model, we help you scale your business.

We help organizations across all industries and sectors; these are our services:
• Team extension: Scale with dedicated consultants on time & material
• Nearshoring as a Service: Leverage a fully scalable development department in Poland 
• Teams: Power up with a dedicated team to help you build, develop and execute your projects
• Managed Services: Let us tailor and manage your projects, delivering on your specific requirements and needs

emagine was founded in 1989 and has a long track record of delivering expertise and business impact for blue-chip companies across Europe.

Today, we are 900+ permanent employees working from departments in 10 countries. Furthermore, we own three state-of-the-art nearshore centres in Poland and one offshore centre in India.

With 40.000 experts in our network and 4500+ partnered consultants on active contracts, we currently help our 500+ clients worldwide with high-end expertise.
descriptionHtml:
Hi,

emagine is looking for a Business Architect to join our client’s cross-functional Digital Procurement team.

You’ll play a key role in translating business needs into scalable technical solutions in close collaboration with developers and business stakeholders across the Source-to-Pay domain. This is a hands-on, high-impact role where you’ll work closely with business functions to map and understand procurement, inventory, and logistics processes – and help shape how they are enabled through our digital platforms and third party systems. You’ll act as a bridge between strategic process understanding and digital development through solution architecture work and planning activities together with the development team.

Required technical skills:

  • Experience with Spring Boot and REST API development
  • Experience building event-driven systems and working with microservices
  • Technocally grounded - ideally with a background in software development/solution architecture

Required domain experience (minimum one of the following):

  • Hands-on experience with Procure-to-Pay (P2P) process implementations (focus: Purchasing, Inventory, Warehouse)
  • ERP system projects in Process industry companies
  • Experience from SAP S/4HANA implementation projects

Profile & skills:

  • Strong communication and process mapping skills – you’re comfortable speaking with business stakeholders and translating their needs
  • Technically grounded – ideally with a background in software development
  • Used to working closely with agile development teams
  • A team player – humble, curious, and easy to collaborate with

If this sounds interesting: get back to me with your CV, a short motivation, availability and your price.

companyDescription:
Sharp Recruitment & Consultants är ett av Sveriges mest specialiserade, erfarna och personliga konsult- och rekryteringsföretag inom juridik, compliance och ekonomi. Vi kombinerar djup branschkunskap med ett exklusivt nätverk av toppkandidater som ofta inte finns på den öppna marknaden. Genom vår skräddarsydda och datadrivna process levererar vi inte bara en kandidat – vi hittar den rätta personen som kan lyfta både företaget och teamet till nästa nivå. Vi är brobyggarna mellan expertis och framgång.

Vi hanterar rekrytering och personaluthyrning på alla nivåer inom privat och offentlig sektor och erbjuder ett komplett utbud av rekryteringsmetoder som anpassas till kundens individuella behov. Vi prioriterar kvalitet och effektivitet högt och arbetar därför alltid i team med en huvudansvarig kontaktperson som ansvarar för hela rekryteringsförfarandet från ax till limpa. Denna process garanterar hög tillgänglighet, anpassningsbarhet och god service för såväl våra uppdragsgivare som kandidater. På Sharp Recruitment & Consultants är vi stolta över vår djupa kunskap och förståelse för marknadens utveckling och ständigt föränderliga behov. Varje organisation är unik och vår uppgift är att på ett ansvarsfullt och närvarande sätt hitta rätt person med relevant kompetens.
companySlogan: Sharp Recruitment & Consultants är en nischad konsult- och rekryteringspartner inom juridik, compliance och ekonomi.
descriptionHtml:
Till ett försäkringsbolag i Stockholm söker vi för kunds räkning en konsult för utredningsarbete avseende AML, CTF samt finansiella sanktioner.


Vi söker dig med minst tre års relevant arbetserfarenhet av att utreda transaktionslarm, inhämta kundkännedom och att självständigt driva utredningar avseende misstänkt penningtvätt.

Vidare ska du ha kunskap och erfarenhet av att utreda ärenden avseende företag och bolagsstrukturer.


Du ska ha en akademisk utbildning med inom juridik, ekonomi eller liknande alternativt gedigen erfarenhet inom området.


Plats: Stockholm, 3-4 dagar på kontoret

Start: Så fort som möjligt

Omfattning: 100%

Längd: Fram till årsskiftet med goda möjligheter till förlängning


För frågor och ytterligare information är du välkommen att kontakta:


Mikaela Virsholm, Affärsområdeschef

+46 76 898 68 38 | mikaela.virsholm@sharprecruitment.se


Rut Bruse, Interim Talent Acquisition Manager

+46 73 410 71 64 | rut.bruse@sharprecruitment.se

companyDescription:
As a leading defence and security company, we offer solutions that range from the depths of the oceans to high in the sky, on land and in cyberspace, to
keep people and society safe.

Empowered by our 22,000 talented people, we constantly push the boundaries of technology to create a safer, more sustainable and more equitable world. We design, manufacture and maintain advanced systems in aeronautics, weapons, command and control, sensors and underwater systems.

We are headquartered in Sweden and have major operations all over the world and are part of the domestic defence capability of several nations.
descriptionHtml:
Vill du påverka och göra skillnad på riktigt? Här bidrar du till att skapa trygghet för människor och samhällen. Det du gör spelar roll.

Letar du efter en möjlighet att arbeta med viktiga frågor inom Saab och visa din kompetens? Detta är en utmärkt chans för dig som vill utveckla etik- och regelefterlevnaden i en högteknologisk och internationell miljö. Vi söker nu en arbetande strateg inom etik- och regelefterlevnad. Här får du chansen att arbeta i en nyckelroll där du bidrar till att säkerställa affärsetiska principer och regelefterlevnad inom Saab.

Din roll

Som medarbetare inom avdelningen Risk och Compliance på Business support, arbetar du nära Dynamics affärsenheter och Saab-koncernens funktioner samt dina kollegor inom business support. Du ansvarar bland annat för att implementera och samordna arbetet kring Saabs ”Code of conduct” och hantering av korruptionsrisker för affärspartners och mellanhänder.

I den här rollen kommer du att ha en central funktion i att leda och vidareutveckla arbetet med etik och regelefterlevnad inom affärsområdet Dynamics. Du säkerställer att Saabs regelverk och processer inte bara implementeras, utan också efterlevs på ett effektivt sätt inom verksamheten.

En viktig del av ditt arbete blir att stödja organisationen i att genomföra korruptionsriskbedömningar, både av affärspartners och av olika affärsupplägg. Du fungerar också som en nyckelperson i kontakt med Market Network Management (MNM), där du ser till att processer för onboarding och förnyelse av tredjepartsaktörer hanteras enhetligt och enligt riktlinjer.

Utöver detta kommer du att vara ett stöd för marknads- och försäljningsorganisationen när det gäller att välja lämpliga marknadsstrategier och affärsupplägg. Du bidrar aktivt till att stärka en kultur av följsamhet och integritet genom att hålla utbildningar, ge daglig rådgivning och fungera som en expertresurs inom etik- och regelefterlevnadsfrågor.

Din profil

För att lyckas i rollen behöver du ha auktoritet, integritet och en god förståelse för internationell affärsverksamhet och dess risker kopplat till etik och regelefterlevnad. Vi ser gärna att du har erfarenhet inom compliance, försäljning eller kommersiella affärer, med god insikt i affärs- och kundbehov.

Vi söker dig som har en mycket god kommunikativ förmåga i både svenska och engelska och som trivs i en dynamisk och internationell miljö. Du har en bred erfarenhet av internationell affärsverksamhet, gärna inom affärsutveckling, marknadsföring och försäljning, kommersiellt arbete eller juridik, och du förstår de affärsmässiga och strategiska aspekterna av regelefterlevnad.

Med en analytisk och lösningsorienterad inställning kan du identifiera och bedöma risker samt ge kvalificerad rådgivning kring affärsupplägg ur ett anti-korruptionsperspektiv. Du har lätt för att bygga förtroendefulla relationer och ser värdet i att skapa och underhålla starka nätverk, både internt och externt.

Som person är du strukturerad, självgående och har förmågan att arbeta självständigt inom ditt expertområde, samtidigt som du samarbetar effektivt med både lokala och koncernövergripande funktioner. Du har ett starkt driv, en uthållighet som gör att du kan hantera komplexa frågeställningar över tid och en vilja att förstå de specifika förutsättningar och risker som varje affärssituation innebär.

I din ansökan ser vi gärna att du bifogar både CV och ett personligt brev, där du tydligt beskriver varför just du är rätt för den här rollen. Berätta hur din erfarenhet, kompetens och driv kan bidra till vårt team och vår framtida utveckling.

Befattningen kräver att du genomgår och godkänns enligt vid var tid gällande bestämmelser för säkerhetsskydd. För befattningar där Saab har krav på säkerhetsklassinplacering kan, i förekommande fall, medföra krav på visst medborgarskap.

Vad du blir en del av

Bakom våra innovationer finns alla de som har gjort det möjligt. Modiga pionjärer och kluriga tänkare. Vardagshjältar och kreativa problemlösare. De som delar djup kunskap och de som utforskar nya områden. Och alla däremellan.

Här har du chansen att påverka och bidra på ditt unika sätt. Allt från att utveckla kod och bygga imponerande försvars- och säkerhetslösningar till att ta en kaffe med en kollega. Varje bidrag räknas. Vi ger dig möjlighet att anta utmaningar och skapa smarta innovationer. I vår vänliga och tekniksmarta värld finns plats för dig att växa. Vi finns här för att tillsammans skapa trygghet för människor och samhällen.

Saab är ett ledande försvars- och säkerhetsföretag med uppdrag att hjälpa nationer att skydda sin befolkning och bidrar till trygghet för människor och samhällen. Med 25,500 talangfulla medarbetare utvecklar Saab teknik och lösningar för en säkrare och mer hållbar värld.

Saab utvecklar, tillverkar och underhåller avancerade system inom flygteknik, vapen, ledningssystem, sensorer och undervattenssystem. Saab har sitt huvudkontor i Sverige men en global verksamhet där Saab är en del av många nationers försvarsförmåga. Läs mer om oss här.

title: Ethics and Compliance officer

companyDescription: As a leading strategy, management and IT consultancy, zeb has been offering transformation expertise along the entire value chain in the financial services sector in Europe since 1992. We have five offices in Germany – Frankfurt, Berlin, Hamburg, Munich and Münster (HQ) – as well as 10 international locations. Our clients include European large-cap and private banks, regional banks, insurers as well as all kinds of financial intermediaries. Several times already, our company has been classed and acknowledged as “best consultancy” for the financial sector in industry rankings.
descriptionHtml:
zeb is one of the leading strategy, management and IT consultancies specializing in financial services in Europe. We support banks, insurance companies and (tech) service providers in dealing with all the challenges and opportunities arising from transformation in the industry.

Your tasks:

  • You analyze how customers' operations are affected by relevant regulations and provide advice on implementation and compliance.
  • You lead and/or support the second-line compliance function at selected customers.
  • You support customers in regulatory-driven change projects or projects to optimize regulatory compliance (including the development of frameworks for internal governance and control, policy documents and organizational structures).
  • You contribute to a healthy culture and an ethical approach among our customers through training.
  • You contribute to the development of our business and customer offering and supervise our junior colleagues.

Your skills:

  • You hold an academic degree in Law or other relevant higher education.
  • You have minimum 4 years of practical experience from a law firm, consultancy, regulatory authority, or a company in the financial or insurance sector.
  • You have experience with or reporting to the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority is a plus.
  • You are a team player with a collaborative and unpretentious approach.
  • You have a strong ability to work independently and you manage complex tasks.
  • You have a high social competence, flexibility, and attention to detail.
  • You have a strong integrity and sound judgment.
  • You are excellent in verbal and written communication skills in both Swedish and English.
  • You are curious, solution-oriented, and driven, with a focus on delivering high-quality results.
  • You have experience in dealing in change and working with complex regulatory matters.
  • You have strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build trust and maintain effective relationships with both clients and colleagues.

Your Benefits

  • Insurances & Pension Schemes: Safely on the road with zeb—in Germany, you are also privately insured via a group accident insurance policy. For business trips abroad, there is an additional travel health insurance and you can also take out a company pension plan with zeb.
  • Job & Family: Do you have questions about the compatibility of consulting and raising a family? In the workingparents@zeb network you will find support and answers.
  • Mobile Work: Thanks to our technical equipment, remote work is possible from anywhere – regardless of whether you are on the train, at home or in the office.
  • zeb.life: Your life is changing—and we would like to accompany you through the different phases. zeb.life offers you support for example in childcare, life situation management and eldercare or in finding concierge services.

Equal opportunities for all: we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds – regardless of age, disability, ethnicity, nationality, gender, religion, sexual identity or social background. As an employer, we rely on people who like to try new things, take responsibility and inspire others through their actions.
